questions.question: Name the group of vascular cryptogams:
questions.explanation: The lower plants that do not form flowers are called cryptogams plant or non-flowering plants. The vascular bundle is absent in these plants but Pteridophytes contain xylem and phloem. Vascular Cryptogam is an old botanical phrase, and it refers to those vascular plants that do not make seeds.  Thus, cryptogam (literally hidden gametophyte) refers to the production of a separate, usually very small, archegoniate gametophyte. Pteridophytes are free-sporing vascular plants that share a unique life cycle with independent gametophyte and sporophyte phases that are frequently discussed together due to common characteristics, including vascular plants.

questions.question: In which class of angiosperm leaves shows parallel venation?
questions.explanation: There are two kinds of venation in leaves of angiosperms plant, parallel venation, and reticulate venation. Monocot plants show parallel venation. If the veins show a more or less parallel disposition, the condition is described as parallel venation. The parallel running veins are often connected at intervals by short links that are placed at right angles to the prominent parallel veins. Parallel venation is characteristic of leaves of monocotyledons.

questions.question: Gymnosperms are characterized by:
questions.explanation: Gymnosperms are those plants that have naked seeds. The seeds are not enclosed inside the ovary. Gymnosperms were the first plants to have seeds. They are often referred to as having naked seeds because they do not have flowers, and their seeds develop on the surface of the reproductive structures of the plants rather than being contained in a specialized ovary. These seeds are often found on the surfaces of cones and short stalks.

questions.question: Sex organs are unicellular and non-jacketed in:
questions.explanation: Algae are the most primitive plants found exclusively in water. The sex organs of algae are unicellular and non-jacketed. The most primitive algae, i.e., the members of Cyanophyceae, e.g., Gloeocapsa, Chroococcus, etc., reproduce by means of fusion, whereas  Nostoc, Anabaena, etc., reproduce vegetatively, by means of a group of few cells called hormogonia, which later on give rise to new plants by further division. Some members of Cyanophyceae reproduce by means of arthrospores. This shows that all the blue-green algae reproduce asexually and the sexual reproduction is altogether absent. In the rest of algae, whether unicellular or filamentous, both asexual and sexual methods of reproduction prevail. It is thought that probably the gametes have been originated from the motile asexual spores or zoospores. Except for a few cases, sexual reproduction has not replaced the asexual method but has been added as a supplementary method. The zoospores usually resemble the gametes except for their size.

questions.question: Amphibians of the plant kingdom are:
questions.explanation: Bryophytes are called amphibians of the plant kingdom because they can survive on both land and water but water is necessary for the completion of the life cycle. Amphibians are those organisms that live on both lands and in water. Bryophytes are called amphibians of the plant kingdom because these plants though live in soil but they need water for sexual reproduction. The sperm of bryophyte (antherozoids) are flagellate and need water to swim to the eggs. In other words, as these plants need water for reproduction unlike other plants, they are called amphibians.

questions.question:
Match the following with correct response.
(1) Thallophyta
(2) Bryophyta
(3) Pteridophyta
(4) Angiosperms

(A) Amphibians of the plant kingdom
(B) Flowering plants
(C) First land plants
(D) Plant body not differentiated
questions.explanation:
Thallophyta A former division of the plant kingdom containing relatively simple non differentiated plant body, i.e. those with no leaves, stems, or roots. It included the algae, bacteria, fungi, and lichens. The thallophytes are a polyphyletic group of non-mobile organisms traditionally described as "thalloid plants", "relatively simple plants" or "lower plants".

 Bryophytes are called amphibians of the plant kingdom because these plants though live in soil but they need water for sexual reproduction. The sperm of bryophyte (antherozoids) are flagellate and need water to swim to the eggs. In other words, as these plants need water for reproduction unlike other plants, they are called as amphibians. 

A pteridophyte are a vascular plant (with xylem and phloem) that reproduces via spores, and therefore was a member of the former and now invalid taxon but first true land plants. Pteridophytes are vascular plants and have leaves (known as fronds), roots and sometimes true stems, and tree ferns have full trunks. Examples include ferns, horsetails and club-mosses. Fronds is the largest species of ferns can reach some six metres in length!

Angiosperms are seed-bearing vascular plants The angiosperms are vascular seed plants in which the ovule (egg) is fertilized and develops into a seed in an enclosed hollow ovary. The ovary itself is usually enclosed in a flower, that part of the angiospermous plant that contains the male or female reproductive organs or both.

questions.question: What is the common name of annelid?
questions.explanation: Annelids are commonly called as segmented worms as their body is divided into small segments. The annelids also are known as the ringed worms or segmented worms. The annelids are bilaterally symmetrical, triploblastic, coelomate invertebrate organisms. They also have parapodia for locomotion. Septa separate the segments of many species but is poorly defined or absent in others.

questions.question: Hirudinaria is the scientific name of
questions.explanation: The scientific name of the leech is Hirudinaria. It is generally found in water and suck blood from animals as food.Leeches are segmented worms that belong to the phylum Annelida and comprise the subclass Hirudinea. Like the earthworms,leeches share a clitellum and are hermaphrodites leeches do not have bristles and the external segmentation of their bodies does not correspond with the internal segmentation of their organs. Their bodies are much more solid as the spaces in their coelom with connective tissues. They also have two suckers, one at each end. The majority of leeches live in freshwater environments, while some species can be found in terrestrial.

questions.question: Name the phyla in which circulatory system is open and body cavity is filled with blood.
questions.explanation:
There are two types of circulatory systems found in animals - Open circulatory system and closed circulatory system. Arthropods and Molluscs have an open circulatory system, where blood fills the body cavity of the animal.
The open circulatory systems (evolved in crustaceans, insects, mollusks, and other invertebrates) pump blood into a hemocoel with the blood diffusing back to the circulatory system between cells. Blood is pumped by a heart into the body cavities, where tissues are surrounded by the blood.

questions.question: Which is the first class of terrestrial chordates?
questions.explanation: Chordates are a group of organisms that contain notochord. They may be aquatic or terrestrial. Reptiles are chordates that mostly live on land. Terrestrial chordate animals are animals that live predominantly or entirely on land e.g., cats, ants, snails, as compared with aquatic animals, which live predominantly or entirely in the water eg. fish, lobsters, or amphibians, which rely on a combination of aquatic and terrestrial habitats.

questions.question: How many cervical vertebrae are found in mammals?
questions.explanation: The vertebral column, also known as the backbone or spine, is part of the axial skeleton. The vertebral column is the defining characteristic of a vertebrate, in which the notochord (a flexible rod of uniform composition) found in all chordates has been replaced by a segmented series of bones vertebrae separated by intervertebral discs. The vertebral column houses the spinal cord, a cavity that encloses and protects the spinal cord. The backbone of mammals consists of the vertebra. There are 7 vertebras in the cervical region of the human body. The vertebrae in the human vertebral column are divided into different regions, which correspond to the curves of the spinal column. The articulating vertebrae are named according to their region of the spine.

questions.question: Which of the following classes have cold blooded animals?
questions.explanation: Those animals whose body temperature change with environmental temperature is called cold-blooded animals. Amphibians and Pisces are cold blood animals. Cold-blooded animals, like reptiles, amphibians, and Pisces, become hotter and colder, depending on the temperature outside. For example, when the sun sets at night, their bodies are cooler because it is less warm outside. When the sun is out, however, their bodies soak up the heat and become warmer Cold-blooded creatures take on the temperature of their surroundings. They are hot when their environment is hot and cold when their environment is cold.

questions.question: How many chambers are present in the heart of fishes?
questions.explanation: The heart of fishes consists of two chambers. The upper chamber is an atrium and the lower chamber is called the ventricle. A rudimentary valve is located between the two chambers. Blood is pumped from the ventricle through the conus arteriosus to the gills. The conus arteriosus is like the aorta in other species. At the gills, the blood receives oxygen and gets rid of carbon dioxide. Blood then moves on to the organs of the body, where nutrients, gases, and wastes are exchanged. There is no division of the circulation between the gills and the body i.e., the blood travels from the heart to the gills, and then directly to the body before returning to the atrium through the sinus venosus to be circulated again. The heart rates of fish fall within the wide range of 60-240 beats per minute.

questions.question: Which one is mismatched?
questions.explanation: Flagella is present in Euglena, pseudopodia are formed by amoeba for capturing food and paramecium has cilia. The malarial parasite plasmodium lacks flagella. They have MYONEMES present in the membrane of plasmodium for movement.

questions.question: Which of the given feature is common among ostrich, penguin, and kiwi?
questions.explanation: Ostrich, penguin, and kiwi are flightless birds that are adapted to different environmental conditions. Flightless birds are birds that through evolution lost the ability to fly. New ecological influences selectively pressured different taxon to converge on flightless modes of existence by altering them morphologically and behaviorally. Two key differences between flying and flightless birds are the smaller wing bones of flightless birds and the absent (or greatly reduced) keel on their breastbone.

questions.question: Elasmobranches do not have:
questions.explanation: Elasmobranches are a kind of marine fish that do not contain Operculum to cover the gills, used for obtaining oxygen from water. Elasmobranchii is a subclass of Chondrichthyes or cartilaginous fish. Members of this subclass are characterized by having five to seven pairs of gill clefts lacking operculum opening individually to the exterior, rigid dorsal fins, and small placoid scales on the skin. elasmobranchs lack swim bladders and maintain buoyancy instead with large livers that are full of oil.

questions.question: The exclusive marine phylum is:
questions.explanation: Echinodermata is exclusively found in seawater or marine. It includes an organism like starfish. Echinoderm is the common name given to any member of the phylum Echinodermata. The adults are recognizable by their (usually five-point) radial symmetry, and include such well-known animals as sea stars, sea urchins as well as the sea lilies or "stone lilies". Echinoderms are found at every ocean depth. Most echinoderms are able to regenerate tissue, organs, limbs, and reproduce sexually; in some cases, they can undergo complete regeneration from a single limb. Geologically, the value of echinoderms is in their ossified skeletons, which are major contributors to many limestone formations, and can provide valuable clues as to the geological environment.

questions.question:
Male Ascaris can be distinguished from female Ascaris by

  1. Curved posterior end
  2. Round shape
  3. Presence of penial setae
questions.explanation:
Male Ascaris differ from female Ascaris in different ways. Female Ascaris are longer in size, male Ascaris have curved posterior end and contain penial setae. 
Ascaris worms are sexually dimorphic, which means that there are differences in body size, shape, color, and so forth between males and females. For example, the males are shorter in length than the females. Male Ascaris worms range from 15-31 cm in length, with the length of female Ascaris worms ranging from 20-49 cm, with an average length of 30 cm. Also, the males have a smaller diameter than females. one of the simplest ways to determine if an Ascaris worm is male or female is the presence or absence of a spicule.

questions.question:
Amphibians are advanced than fishes because of

  1. Three chambered heart
  2. lungs for respiration
questions.explanation:
Amphibians are more advanced than fishes because of three-chambered hearts and lungs for respiration in place of gills.
Amphibians have three-chambered heart and lungs for respiration, while fishes have two-chambered heart and gills for respiration. Well, they have limbs and lungs, which consider being more complex. fish, amphibians, and reptiles are water creatures, so when they breathe the water cleans their air supply, which makes their immune system weak air breathers are used to the pollution which means they have a stronger immune system.

questions.question: Which of the following is Hemichordate?
questions.explanation: Hemichordates are that organism that lacks notochord and true dorsal nerve. Balanoglossus is an example of a hemichordate phylum. Hemichordata is a phylum of marine deuterostome animals, generally considered the sister group of the echinoderms. They appear in the Lower or Middle Cambrian and include two main classes: Enteropneusta (acorn worms), and Pterobranchia They are Solitary and colonial, mostly tubicolous, exclusively marine. Body soft, fragile, vermiform, and divisible into proboscis, collar, and trunk. Body wall with a single-layered epidermis.

questions.question: Whales are not grouped in fishes because:
questions.explanation: The whale is found in water but they respire with lungs, have a four-chambered heart, and possess mammary glands, so not placed in fishes. Whales are mammals because they give birth to live young, they have fur (although it is very sparse on their body), they have lungs and breathe air and they provide milk for their young. Whales are warm-blooded, which means they keep a high body temperature that does not change in the cold water. Fish are cold-blooded, so their body temperature changes depending on the temperature of their environment. Whales actually breathe air with lungs using their blowholes to breathe out! They come to the surface of the water so they can breathe just like you and me. Fish get oxygen directly from the water through their gills. Whales even have a little bit of hair on their smooth skin, usually on the top of their head. Fish have scales.

questions.question: Which of the following is known as cuttlefish?
questions.explanation: Cuttlefish are marine animals of the order Sepia. They belong to class Cephalopoda that includes Sepia. Cuttlefish have an internal shell (called a cuttlebone), large eyes, and eight arms and two tentacles furnished with finely toothed suckers, with which they grab their prey Cuttlefish have an internal cuttlebone, made of calcium carbonate. It is porous, full of small holes, The buoyancy of the cuttlebone can change, allowing the cuttlefish to go lower or higher by changing the amount of gas and liquid in its chambers. The cuttlebone of each species has a distinct shape, size, and pattern of ridges or texture. Cuttlebones are used by jewelers and silversmiths as molds for casting small objects.

questions.question: ________ has a two-chambered heart.
questions.explanation: The members of family Pisces have two-chambered heart, one atrium, and one ventricle. Rohu has a two-chambered heart out of the organism given in the options. The 2-chambered heart is an interesting form of the primary circulatory organ and exists primarily in animals that use gills for breathing. The two types of chambers are the atria - receiving chambers - and ventricles - pumping chambers. the atria are the first stops for blood entering the heart, and the ventricles are the chambers that push blood out of the organ. The organization of these chambers will differ depending on the needs of the system, particularly as it relates to breathing and respiration. Animals with 2-chambered hearts are those that use gills to replenish oxygen in the blood supply,

questions.question: Identify the animal group having 4 pairs of jointed legs and no wings.
questions.explanation: The animals belonging to group Arachnida have 4 pairs of jointed legs and no wings to fly. Arachnids have joint legged invertebrates with no wings. All arachnids have eight legs, although the front pair of legs in some species has converted to a sensory function, while in other species, different appendages can grow large enough to take on the appearance of extra pairs of legs. Almost all extant arachnids are terrestrial, living mainly on land. However, some inhabit freshwater environments arachnids have an exoskeleton, and they also have an internal structure of cartilage-like tissue, called the endosternite, to which certain muscle groups are attached.

questions.question: Which of the following is not the flight adaptation in birds?

options.html: Presence of air sacs to help in double respiration
Thin, moist. Glandular and respiratory skin

questions.explanation: Birds are adapted to fly in the air by having well-developed flight muscles, presence of air sacs, and having an acute sharp vision. They also have thin, moist, glandular, and respiratory skin but not adaptation for flight. Many of a bird's bones are hollow which makes birds' bodies lightweight. Flying birds have large chest muscles that move the wings. Birds have feathers that help them fly. The long flight feathers on the wings and tail help birds balance and steer.

questions.question: ‘Venus flower basket’ is the dried skeleton of:
questions.explanation: The Venus flower basket is the dried skeleton of Euplectella, which is a hexactinellid sponge in the phylum Porifera. The Venus' flower basket (Euplectella aspergillum) is a hexactinellid sponge in the phylum Porifera inhabiting the deep ocean. They breed, and when their offspring are tiny, the offspring escape to find a Venus' flower basket of their own. The shrimp inside the basket clean it and, in return, the basket provides food for the shrimp by trapping it in its tissues and then releasing wastes into the body of the sponge for the shrimp.

questions.question: Mesoglea is characteristic of:
questions.explanation: Mesoglea is characteristic of phylum Cnidaria. Mesoglea, also known as mesohyl, is the translucent, non-living, jelly-like substance found between the two epithelial cell layers (i.e., between the ectoderm and endoderm) in the bodies of cnidarians and sponges. The mesoglea also contains wandering amoebocytes that play a role in phagocytosing debris and bacteria. These cells also fight infections by producing antibacterial chemicals mesoglea may be thinner than either of the cell layers in smaller coelenterates like a hydra or may make up the bulk of the body in larger jellyfish. The mesoglea serves as an internal skeleton, supporting the body. Its elastic properties help restore the shape after it is deformed by the contraction of muscles.

questions.question: Which phylum contains the greatest number of species of animals?
questions.explanation: The phylum arthropods contain the largest number of species in animals, which includes all insects. Arthropoda is the largest phylum in the animal kingdom, which includes such familiar forms as lobsters, crabs, spiders, mites, insects, centipedes, and millipedes. About 84 percent of all known species of animals are members of this phylum. Arthropods are represented in every habitat on Earth and show a great variety of adaptations. Several types live in aquatic environments, and others reside in terrestrial ones; some groups are even adapted for flight. The distinguishing feature of arthropods is the presence of a jointed skeletal covering composed of chitin (a complex sugar) bound to protein. This nonliving exoskeleton is secreted by the underlying epidermis (which corresponds to the skin of other animals). Arthropods lack locomotory cilia, even in the larval stages, probably because of the presence of the exoskeleton. The body is usually segmented, and the segments bear paired, jointed appendages, from which the name arthropod (“jointed feet”) is derived.

questions.question:
Match the following with correct response.

(a) Amphibians (i) Eggs with tough covering
(b) Reptiles (ii) Viviparous
(c) Aves (iii) Egg laying
(d) Mammals (iv) Lay eggs in water
questions.explanation:
• Amphibians are cold-blooded vertebrates (vertebrates have backbones) that don't have scales. They live part of their lives in water and part on land amphibians are ectothermic, tetrapod vertebrates. 
  • Reptiles are tetrapod (four-limbed vertebrate) animals in the class Reptilia, comprising today's turtles, crocodilians. Reptiles are cold-blooded vertebrates. (Vertebrates have backbones.) They have dry skin covered with scales or bony plates and usually lay soft-shelled eggs.
  • Aves is the Latin name for the birds - feathered, winged, bipedal, warm-blooded, egg-laying, vertebrate animals with evolutionary origins. Birds (Aves) are a group of endothermic vertebrates, characterized by feathers, toothless beaked jaws, the laying of hard-shelled eggs, a high metabolic rate.
  • Mammals are any vertebrates within the class Mammalia a clade of endothermic amniotes distinguished from reptiles by the possession of a neocortex, hair, three middle ear bones, and mammary glands Mammals include humans and all other animals that are warm-blooded vertebrates (vertebrates have backbones) with hair. They feed their young with milk.

questions.question:
Match the following with correct response.

(a) Peacock (i) Muscadomestica
(b) Tiger (ii) Pavocristatus
(c) Housefly (iii) Ranatigrina
(d) Frog (iv) Pantheratigris
questions.explanation:
• Pavo cristatus is the scientific name of peacock according to binomial nomenclature.
  • The tiger is the largest cat species, most recognizable for their pattern of dark vertical stripes on reddish-orange fur with a lighter underside. The species is classified in the genus Panthera.
  • The housefly  Musca domestica is a fly of the suborder Cyclorrhapha. It is believed to have evolved in the Cenozoic era, possibly in the Middle East, and has spread all over the world. It is the most common fly species found inhabitations.
  • A frog has Rana tigris as the scientific name is a member of a diverse and largely carnivorous group of short-bodied, tailless amphibians composing the order Anura.

tests.name: Diversity in Living Organisms Test-04
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Diversity in Living Organisms Test-04 for CBSE class 09 Science. Students are instructed o read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question:
Match the following with correct response.

(a) Porifera (i) Metameric animals
(b) Platyhelminthes (ii) Lacks Nervous system
(c) Annelida (iii) Largest phylum
(d) Arthropoda (iv) First triploblastic
questions.explanation:
Sponges are the basalmost clade of animals of the phylum Porifera. They are multicellular Sponges do not have nervous, digestive or circulatory systems. Unlike other animals, they lack true tissues and organs,
Platyhelminthes are the simplest animals that are bilaterally symmetrical and triploblastic composed of three fundamental cell layers. Flatworms have no body cavity other than the gut (and the smallest free-living forms may even lack that!) and lack an anus; the same pharyngeal opening both takes in food and expels waste.
Annelid's body is metameric. bilaterally symmetrical, and wormlike. They show protostome characteristics include spiral cleavage. trochophore larvae and schiz coelous coelom formation. They have paired epidermal setae. They closed the circulatory system.
An arthropod is an invertebrate animal having an exoskeleton, a segmented body, and paired jointed appendages. Arthropods form the phylum Arthropoda, which includes insects, arachnids.

questions.question:
Match the following with correct response.

(a) Annelida (i) Malpighian tubules
(b) Arthropoda (ii) Kidney
(c) Platyhelminthes (iii) Flame cells
(d) Mammals (iv) Nephridia
questions.explanation:
• Annelids have the nephridium (plural nephridia) as an invertebrate organ that occurs in pairs and performs a function similar to the vertebrate kidney. Nephridia remove metabolic wastes from an animal's body. They are present in many different invertebrate lines.
  • Arthropods have the Malpighian tubule system is a type of excretory and osmoregulatory system found in some insects, myriapods, arachnids, and tardigrades. The system consists of branching tubules extending from the alimentary canal that absorbs solutes, water, and wastes from the surrounding hemolymph.
  • A flame cell is a specialized excretory cell found in the simplest freshwater invertebrates, including flatworms (except the turbellarian order Acoela), rotifers, and nemerteans; these are the simplest animals to have a dedicated excretory system. Bundles of flame cells are called protonephridia.
  • Mammals have kidneys as their excretory organs. The kidneys excrete a variety of waste products produced by metabolism into the urine. These include the nitrogenous wastes urea, from protein catabolism, and uric acid, from nucleic acid metabolism.

questions.question:
Respiration in arthropods occurs through

  1. Trachea
  2. Booklungs
  3. Gills
  4. Skin
questions.explanation: Aquatic arthropods (crustaceans and the chelicerate horseshoe crabs) possess gills for respiration. Although they vary in structure and location, the gills are always outgrowths of the integument (skin) and are therefore covered by the exoskeleton, which is thin in this area and not a barrier to the exchange of gases. Terrestrial arthropods possess tracheae and book lungs as respiratory organs. Tracheae are a system of tiny tubes that permit the passage of gases into the interior of the body. In some arthropods, the tracheal tubes are bathed by blood, but in insects, the minute terminal endings (tracheoles) are embedded in the tissues, even within muscle cells.

questions.question:
Which of the following is not a true fish:

  1. Jellyfish
  2. Starfish
  3. Dogfish
  4. Silverfish
questions.explanation:
1. Silverfish belongs to Arthropoda phyla-Not a fish.
  2. Jellyfish belongs to Cnidarian-Not a fish.
  3. Dogfish belongs to Pisces so it is a true fish.
  4. Starfish belongs to Echinodermata- Not a fish.

questions.question:
Which of the following is not a flightless bird?

  1. Ostrich
  2. Kiwi
  3. Dove
  4. Pigeon
questions.explanation: Flightless birds are birds that through evolution lost the ability to fly. New ecological influences selectively pressured different taxon to converge on flightless modes of existence by altering them morphologically and behaviorally. It is believed that the loss of flight is an easier transition for birds rather than the loss and regain of flight, which has never been documented in avian history. The wings are large and have eleven primary feathers and low wing loading pigeons have strong wing muscles and are among the strongest fliers of all birds. Columbidae has unique body feathers, with the shaft being generally broad, strong, and flattened, tapering to a fine point, abruptly.

questions.question:
Which of the following is incorrect-

  1. Order- A group of related families
  2. Class – A group of order
  3. Family – A group of related species
  4. Genus – A group of related family
questions.explanation:
In biological classification, family (Latin: familia, plural familiae) is one of the eight major taxonomic ranks; it is classified between order and genus. A family may be divided into subfamilies, which are intermediate ranks above the rank of genus. the Systema Naturae, Carl Linnaeus used a ranking scale limited to kingdom, class, order, genus, species, and one rank below species.
A genus is a taxonomic rank used in the biological classification of living and fossil organisms in biology. In the hierarchy of biological classification, the genus comes above species and below a family. In binomial nomenclature, the genus name forms the first part of the binomial species name for each species within the genus.

questions.question:
Which of the following are characteristics of mammals?
A Cold- blooded animals
B Viviparous
C Exoskeleton of hair
D Respiration by gills
questions.explanation: Mammals are vertebrates (which means they have a backbone or spine). Also known as “warm-blooded,” endothermic animals regulate their own body temperate which allows them to live in almost every climate on Earth. Have hair on their bodies. Mammals are viviparous as they Produce milk to feed their babies

questions.question:
Find the incorrect pair:

  1. Wheat – Solanum tuberosum
  2. Cat – Felis domesticus
  3. Human – Homo sapiens
  4. Mango – Mangifera indica
questions.explanation: The scientific name of organisms consists of two words, the genus, and species name. The biological name of wheat is Triticum aestivum. Solanum tuberosum is the biological name of the potato.

questions.question: What is the scientific name of the national bird of India?
questions.explanation: The national bird of India is a peacock, whose scientific name is Pavo cristatus. The peafowl includes three species of birds in the genera Pavo and Afropavo of the Phasianidae family, the pheasants, and their allies. Male peafowl is known for their piercing call and their extravagant plumage. The latter is especially prominent in the Asiatic species, who have an eye-spotted "tail" or "train" of covert feathers which they display as part of a courtship ritual. The term peacock is properly reserved for the male; the female is known as a peahen.

questions.question:
Match the following with correct response.

(a) Marchantia (i) Pteridophyta
(b) Marsilea (ii) Gymnosperms
(c) Cladophora (iii) Thallophyta
(d) Pinus (iv) Bryophyta
questions.explanation:
• Marchantia is a genus in the family Marchantiaceae of the order Marchantiales, a group of liverworts. The Marchantia shows differentiation into two layers: an upper assimilatory region and a lower storage region with a well-defined upper epidermis with air channels. It is variable in appearance and has several subspecies. It is dioicous, having separate male and female plants.
  • Marsilea quadrifolia is a herbaceous plant found naturally in central and southern Europe These small pteridophytes are of unusual appearance and do not resemble common ferns. Common names include water clover and four-leaf clover because the long-stalked leaves have four clover-like lobes and are either held above water or submerged.
  • Cladophores are thallophytes. The thallus is unicellular and colonial. Colonies are jelly, like coloured yellowish to brownish. Cells are spherical to ovoid and are surrounded by well-defined, thick, stratified envelopes (sheaths) and thus forming colonial appearance. Each cell contains granular cytoplasm without any demarcation of organelles and nuclei.
  • A pine is any conifer in the genus Pinus, of the family Pinaceae. Pinus is the sole genus in the subfamily Pinoideae belongs to gymnosperms which have naked seeds Gymnosperms are seed-bearing vascular plants, such as cycads, ginkgo, yews, and conifers, in which the ovules or seeds are not enclosed in an ovary. The word "gymnosperm" comes from the Greek word gymnospermos, meaning "naked seeds."

questions.question:
Match the following with correct response.

(a) Pea (i) Triticumaestivum
(b) Onion (ii) Pisumsativum
(c) Wheat (iii) Mangifera Indica
(d) Mango (iv) Allium cepa
questions.explanation:
• Pisum sativum is the binomial name of Pea.
  • Allium cepa is the scientific name for Onion.
  • Triticum sativum is the scientific name of Wheat.
  • Mangifera indica is the binomial name for mango according to the biological nomenclature.

questions.question: Name the organism in which asexual reproduction takes place by budding.
questions.explanation: Lower organisms reproduce asexually to produce similar offspring. In hydra asexual reproduction takes place by budding method. Hydra genus of small, fresh-water animals of the phylum Cnidaria and class Hydrozoa. They are native to the temperate and tropical regions When food is plentiful, many Hydra reproduce asexually by producing buds in the body wall, which grow to be miniature adults and simply break away when they are mature.

questions.question:
Find the correct statement

  1. Plasmodium is a pathogenic human parasite.
  2. Order is an assemblage of related classes.
  3. The red and brown algae do not contain any chlorophyll.
  4. Blue whale is the largest known fish.
questions.explanation:
• Related orders form a class. Red and brown algae contain chlorophyll besides other pigments. The blue whale is not a fish but plasmodium is a pathogenic human parasite that causes malaria.
  • The blue whale is a marine mammal belonging to the baleen whales. At up to 29.9 metres in length and with a maximum recorded weight of 173 tonnes and probably reaching over 181 tonnes, it is the largest animal known to have ever existed and these are the mammals.
  • Plasmodium, a genus of parasitic protozoans of the sporozoan subclass Coccidia that are the causative organisms of malaria. Plasmodium, which infects red blood cells in mammals (including humans), birds, and reptiles, occurs worldwide, especially in tropical and temperate zones.

questions.question: The common name of Ascaris is-
questions.explanation: The common name of Ascaris is roundworm due to the cylindrical shape of the body. They are exclusively endoparasite. Ascaris is a genus of parasitic nematode worms known as the "small intestinal roundworms", which is a type of helminth (parasitic worm). Ascariasis is a disease caused by the parasitic roundworm Ascaris lumbricoides whose eggs are extremely resistant to strong chemicals, desiccation, and low temperatures.

questions.question:
Match the following with correct response.

(a) Naked seed (i) Angiosperms
(b) Covered seed (ii) Paramecium
(c) Flagella (iii) Euglena
(d) Cilia (iv) Gymnosperms
questions.explanation:
• Gymnosperm, any vascular plant that reproduces by means of an exposed seed, or ovule—unlike angiosperms, or flowering plants, whose seeds are enclosed by mature ovaries, or fruits. The seeds of many gymnosperms (literally “naked seeds”) are borne in cones and are not visible
  • Angiosperms are seed-bearing vascular plants. Their reproductive structures are flowers in which the ovules are enclosed in an ovary. The angiosperms are vascular seed plants in which the ovule (egg) is fertilized and develops into a seed in an enclosed hollow ovary. The ovary itself is usually enclosed in a flower, that part of the angiospermous plant that contains the male or female reproductive organs or both.
  • A flagellum is a locomotive organ of euglena is a whip-like structure that allows a cell to move. They are found in all three domains of the living world: bacteria, archaea, and Eukaryota, also known as protists, plants, animals, and fungi. While all three types of flagella are used for locomotion, they are structurally very different.
  • A cilium locomotive organ of paramoecium is an organelle found in eukaryotic cells. Cilia are slender protuberances that project from the much larger cell body. There are two types of cilia: motile cilia and nonmotile, or primary cilia, which typically serve as sensory organelles.

tests.name: Motion Test-01
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Motion Test-01 for CBSE class 09 Science. Students are instructed o read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question:
Which of the following is a vector quantity?

  1. Retardation
  2. Speed
  3. Force
  4. Distance
questions.explanation:
Acceleration is a vector quantity.
Since retardation and force are related to acceleration. Therefore, they are vector quantities.

questions.question:
Which of the is true for displacement?

  1. It cannot be zero.
  2. Its magnitude is greater than the distance travelled by the object.
  3. It’s the shortest distance between initial & final position.
  4. It is a vector quantity.
questions.explanation:
• When a body moves from one position to another, the shortest (straight line) distance between the initial position and final position of the body is known as its displacement. It's a vector quantity.
  • Displacement can be zero, less than the magnitude of distance but can never be more than the magnitude of distance covered.

questions.question:
Statement A: When a body moves with uniform speed, then the average speed is the same as instantaneous speed.
Statement B: Average speed is less than the average velocity.
Which of the above statement is true?
questions.explanation:
A body traveling with uniform speed travels equal distances in equal intervals of time. Hence, both the average speed and instantaneous speed are the same.
The average speed is the total distance travelled divided by the total time taken by the object for cover up to that distance. Average velocity is the total displacement of objects divided by the total time taken by the object to cover up that distance.
So, Statement A is true and Statement B is false.

questions.question: A car is moving with a uniform speed and covers 100 m in 50 second. Find the speed of the car.
questions.explanation:
Speed = {tex}\frac{distan ce}{time}{/tex} 
Here, distance = 100 m and time = 50 s.
Therefore,
Speed = {tex}\frac{100}{50}{/tex} = 2 ms-1

questions.question: Instantaneous speed of a vehicle is measured by
questions.explanation: The device used to measure the instantaneous speed of the vehicle is the speedometer. The speedometer of a car reveals information about the instantaneous speed of your car. It shows your speed at a particular instant in time.

questions.question: A particle covers equal distances in equal intervals of time. It is said to be-
questions.explanation: If a body covers equal distance in equal intervals of time then it is said to be moving at a uniform speed. Mathematically speed is defined as v =  {tex}\frac St{/tex} , where v is the speed of the object, S is the distance covered and t is the time taken.

questions.question:
Which of the statement is true?
Statement A: A curved line in a speed-time graph means non-uniform motion.
Statement B: A curved line in a velocity-time graph means uniform acceleration.

options.html: Neither statement A nor Statement B is true.
Statement A is false, B is true
Statement A is true, B is false
Both the statement A and B are true

questions.explanation:
When the speed of a body changes in an irregular manner, the speed-time graph of the body is a curved line. The distance traveled by the body is given by the area between the speed-time curve and the time axis. For uniform speed graph is a straight line. For non-uniform speed graph is a curved line. For Increasing speed graph  Straight line sloping upwards. For decreasing speed graph Straight line sloping downwards.
When the velocity-time graph is plotted for an object moving with uniform acceleration, the slope of the graph is a straight line.
So, statement A is true and B is false.

questions.question: A train 120m long moving on a straight and level track with uniform speed passes a pole in 6 seconds. Find the time it will take to cross a 50 m long bridge.
questions.explanation:
The speed of train = {tex}120 \over6{/tex}m = 20 m/s.
Time taken to cross a 50 m long bridge = {tex}(120 + 50 \ )\over20{/tex} = {tex}170\over20{/tex} = 8.5 second.

questions.question:
Match the following with correct response.

(1) Speed (A) {tex}\frac{Displacement}{Time\;taken}​\;{/tex}
(2) Velocity (B){tex}\frac{total\;distance\;covred}{total\;time\;taken\;}​\;{/tex}
(3) Acceleration (C) {tex}\frac{Change\;in\;velocity}{Time\;taken}​\;{/tex}
(4) Average speed (D) {tex}\frac{Distance\;travelled}{Time\;take}​\;{/tex}
questions.explanation:
1. Distance travelled per unit time is called speed. SI unit for speed is m/s. Speed ={tex} \frac{Distance\;travelled}{Time\;take}​\;{/tex}
  2. Displacement per unit time is known as velocity. It is nothing but speed of an object in a definite direction. Velocity = {tex}\frac{Displacement}{Time\;taken}​\;{/tex}
  3. Rate of change of velocity with time is known as acceleration. SI unit of acceleration is m/s2. Acceleration = {tex}\frac{Change\;in\;velocity}{Time\;taken}​\;{/tex}
  4. The ratio of total distance travelled to the total time taken by the body gives its average speed. Average speed = {tex} \frac{total\;distance\;covred}{total\;time\;taken\;}​\;{/tex}

questions.question:
Which of the following is an example of uniform motion?

  1. The motion of the car on a busy road.
  2. The motion of a satellite around the planet.
  3. The motion of the moon around the earth.
  4. The motion of any particle on the arm of a clock.
questions.explanation:
• Uniform Motion means Body covers equal distance in an equal time interval.
  • A satellite goes around the earth in a circular orbit with constant speed and covers equal distances in equal time intervals.
  • The arms of the clock cover the equal distance in every hour.
  • The moon revolves around the earth and takes equal time for every revolution.
  • The motion of the car on a busy road is not an example of a uniform motion.

questions.question: Which of the following physical quantity is not present in the first equation of motion?
questions.explanation:
The first equation of motion is v = u + at, where v is the final velocity and u is the initial velocity of the body and a is uniform acceleration.
The first equation of motion gives velocity acquired by the body at any time t.
So, in the first equation of motion displacement is not present.

questions.question: Which of the following is the case of accelerated motion?

options.html: The motion of the athletes in a straight line.
Both uniform circular motion and motion of the athletes in a straight line.

questions.explanation:
The motion of the athletes in a straight line and uniform circular motion both are the case of accelerated motion. The speed of the athletes goes on increasing.
The velocity of a body in uniform circular motion changes due to continuous change in direction, therefore, the motion along a circular path is said to be accelerated.

questions.question: What is the quantity which is measured as the area occupied by the velocity-time graph?
questions.explanation:
In a velocity-time graph, the area enclosed by the velocity-time curve, and the time-axis gives us the displacement of the body.
In a velocity-time graph:

  1. The slope of the graph gives the acceleration of the body. Finding the slope of the graph gives its acceleration.
  2. The area under the velocity-time graph gives the displacement of the object.

questions.question: What is the nature of the distance-time graph for the uniform motion of an object?
questions.explanation: When the slope of the distance-time graph is a straight line parallel to the time axis, the object is moving with uniform motion. As in uniform motion, the distance-time graph would be a straight line, because the equal distance is covered in equal units of time.

questions.question: What can you say about the motion of an object whose distance-time graph is a straight line parallel to time-axis?

options.html: object is moving with constant speed

questions.explanation: If a body is at rest, then as the time passes, there is no change in the distance travelled by the body. Hence, the distance-time graph of a body at rest is a horizontal line parallel to time axis.

tests.name: Motion Test-02
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Motion Test-02 for CBSE class 09 Science. Students are instructed o read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: A body moves in a uniform circular motion because:

options.html: it is moving with Decreasing velocity.
it is moving with constant velocity.

questions.explanation: When a body moves in a uniform circular motion then the body has an accelerated motion. It is because of a change in velocity due to continuous change in direction of motion.

questions.question: 180o plane angle is equal to:
questions.explanation:
360° = 2{tex}\pi{/tex} radian
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{360^o}2{/tex}= {tex}\pi{/tex} radian
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 180 = {tex}\pi{/tex} radian

questions.question: A body is travelling at a constant speed in a circle, It has:
questions.explanation: The body moves with the constant speed it means that tangential acceleration = 0 and only centripetal acceleration exists whose directions are always towards the center or inwards.

questions.question:
Statement A: Uniform circular motion is a case of accelerated motion.
Statement B: In the third equation of motion we do not have the time.
Which of the following statements are correct:

options.html: Both statements A and B are true
Statement A is true, B is false
Neither statement A nor statement B is true.
Statement B is true, A is false

questions.explanation:
Uniform circular motion is a case of accelerated motion. When a body moves in a uniform circular motion then the body has an accelerated motion. It is because of a change in velocity due to continuous change in direction of motion.
The third{tex}{/tex} equation of motion, v2 = u2 + 2aS .In this equation of motion, time is not present.
So, both statements are true.

questions.question: If two objects move in a circular path of radii in the ratio of 1:3 and take some time to complete the circle, what is the ratio of their speeds?
questions.explanation:
Let the radius of circular path be r and 3r respectively.
Let the speed of first and second objects be v1 and v2 respectively.
The length of the first track = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}r
Time is taken by the first object = {tex}\frac{2\pi r}{v_1}{/tex}
The length of the second track  = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}(3r) = 6 {tex}\pi{/tex}r
Time is taken by the second object t = {tex}\frac{6\pi r}{v_2}{/tex}
Time taken by both object are the same
{tex}\frac{2\pi r}{v_1}{/tex} ={tex}\frac{6\pi r}{v_2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac2{v_1}=\frac{\displaystyle6}{\displaystyle v_2}{/tex} 
{tex}\frac{v_1}{v_2}=\;\frac13{/tex}

questions.question:
Which of the following is correct about uniform circular motion?

  1. The direction of motion is continuously changed.
  2. The direction of motion is not changed.
  3. Speed and direction both remain constant.
  4. Speed is constant, but the direction is changing.
questions.explanation: In a uniform circular motion, the direction of the body continuously changes, and hence the velocity (speed) of the body also changes.

questions.question: Which of the quantities - speed, time, velocity and acceleration have the same SI unit?
questions.explanation: SI unit of both speed and velocity is m/s. Although velocity is a vector quantity speed is a scalar quantity.

questions.question:
Find the correct statement:

  1. The SI unit of retardation is -ms-2
  2. The motion of the athletes is uniform
  3. Displacement is a scalar quantity
  4. Velocity has magnitude only
questions.explanation:
If there is a decrease in acceleration, it is called Retardation. This means the rate of decrease in velocity is called Retardation.  The negative acceleration is termed retardation or deceleration.
SI unit of acceleration (a) is m/s2 or ms-2.
So, the unit of retardation is -ms-2.

questions.question: A body is said to be in rest when:

options.html: Its position doesn’t change with time with respect to the observer.
The body moves in uniform motion, w.r.t observer.
It's position changes with time w.r.t observer.

questions.explanation: When a body does not change its position with respect to time, it is said that body is in a state of rest. Rest and motion are relative to the observer.

questions.question: A person sitting in the truck projected a ball vertically upwards. The ball:

options.html: falls in front of the truck
falls by the side of truck

questions.explanation: A person sitting in the truck projected a ball vertically upwards. The ball will fall back in his hand as the ball and the truck are moving at the same speed.

questions.question: Find the incorrect statement

options.html: The body is said to be accelerating if it moves in a uniform circular motion
When a body moves with constant speed its acceleration is zero
The slope of velocity-time graph gives instantaneous acceleration

questions.explanation:
If a body with constant speed is travelling in the same direction(i.e. it is not changing its direction) then its velocity is constant and so its acceleration will be zero. But if the object is changing its direction then its velocity is also changing and so it possesses the acceleration.
Hence, the given statement is incorrect.

questions.question:
Match the following with the correct response.

(a) Scalar (i) Magnitude and direction
(b) Vector (ii) Motion of satellite
(c) Rectilinear motion (iii) Non-uniform
(d) Circular motion (iv) Magnitude
questions.explanation:
• Scalar- A physical quantity having only magnitude is known as a scalar quantity. A scalar quantity has no direction.
  • Vector- A physical quantity having magnitude as well as direction is known as a vector quantity.
  • Circular motion - Motion about a fixed point called the centre. In other words, motion in a circle is circular motion.
  • Rectilinear motion- Rectilinear motion is another name for straight-line motion. This type of motion describes the movement of a particle or a body.

questions.question:
Match the following with correct response.

Column A Column B
(1) Velocity (A) {tex}m/{s^2}{/tex}
(2) Displacement (B){tex} m/s{/tex}
(3) Acceleration (C) {tex} -m/{s^2}{/tex}
(4) Retardation (D) {tex}m{/tex}
questions.explanation:
1. The SI unit of velocity is meter per second.
  2. The SI unit of acceleration is the metre per second square.
  3. The SI unit of displacement is the meter.
  4. Retardation is negative acceleration.Therefore its SI unit is metre per second squared with a negative sign.

questions.question:
Match the following with the correct response.

(a) Uniform acceleration (i) Vector quantity
(b) Measurement of distance (ii) Odometer
(c) Energy (iii) Velocity increases in equal intervals of time
(d) Acceleration (iv) Scalar quantity
questions.explanation:
Vector- A physical quantity having magnitude as well as direction is known as a vector quantity.
Odometer- an instrument for measuring the distance travelled by a wheeled vehicle.
Acceleration of a body - It is defined as the rate of change of its velocity with time.
Scalar- A physical quantity having only magnitude is known as a scalar quantity.

questions.question:
Match the following with the correct response.

(a) v (i) {tex}\frac{{v - u}}{t}{/tex}
(b) a (ii) v2 − u2
(c) Vav (iii) {tex}\frac{{v + u}}{2}{/tex}
(d) 2aS (iv) {tex}\frac{s}{t}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
• The general formula of the quantities acceleration, speed, average velocity, and third equation of motion is given.
  • The velocity of a body is defined as the rate of change of its displacement with time,{tex}v= \frac{s}{t}{/tex}.
  • Acceleration of a body is defined as the rate of change of its velocity with time,{tex}a= \frac{v-u}{t}{/tex}. 
  • The average velocity is given by the "arithmetic mean" of the initial and final velocity for a given period of time, that is Average velocity,{tex}V_{av}= \frac{v+u}{2}{/tex}.
  • Third equation of motion: v2 = u2 + 2aS {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} v2 − u2 = 2aS.

tests.name: Motion Test-03
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Motion Test-03 for CBSE class 09 Science. Students are instructed o read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: The area under the velocity-time graph gives:
questions.explanation:
In a velocity-time graph, the area enclosed by the velocity-time curve, and the time-axis gives us the displacement of the body.
In a velocity-time graph:

  1. The slope of the graph gives the acceleration of the body. Finding the slope of the graph gives its acceleration.
  2. The area under the velocity-time graph gives the displacement of the object.

questions.question: Which of the following velocity-time graph represents uniform acceleration?
questions.explanation: In the velocity-time graph as velocity changes at a constant rate with respect to time in uniformly accelerating motion, the graph would be a straight line with its slope indicating the amount of acceleration.

questions.question:
Which of the following is a false statement?

  1. The body can have a constant velocity and still have a varying speed.
  2. The body can have zero velocity and still be accelerated.
  3. The body can have a constant speed and still have varying velocity
  4. The direction of the velocity of a body can change when its acceleration is constant.
questions.explanation: If velocity is constant then the speed has to be constant. A body can have a constant speed but a changing velocity because the direction can change while the speed is constant. (A car goes 50 mph around a roundabout). However, a body cannot have a constant velocity at changing speed.

questions.question: m/s2 is the SI unit of:
questions.explanation: The rate of change of velocity with time is known as acceleration. SI unit of acceleration is m/s2. Acceleration = {tex} \frac{Change\;in\;velocity}{Time\;taken}​\; {/tex}

questions.question: The below graph represents
questions.explanation: In the speed-time graph shown above, speed is decreasing with time. This type of graph is represented by non – uniform retardation.

questions.question: Which of the following situations are not possible?

options.html: A body moving horizontally with acceleration in the vertical direction
A body moving with a constant velocity in an accelerated motion
A body moving with constant acceleration but with zero velocity.
The area enclosed by velocity-time graph and time axis gives the magnitude of displacement.

questions.explanation:
No, the object can’t move with constant velocity in accelerated motion. A body moving with a constant velocity in an accelerated motion is possible in a uniform circular motion.
When a body tied with rope moves in a vertical circular path, at the bottom-most point velocity is horizontal and acceleration is in the vertical direction. So,A body moving horizontally with acceleration in the vertical direction is possible.
When an object is thrown in an upward direction, at the maximum height the velocity of the object becomes zero but still in that condition a constant acceleration due to gravity is working. So, a body moving with constant acceleration but with zero velocity is possible.
In velocity-time graphs; the regions between the line and the time-axis represent the displacement during the stated time interval.

questions.question: If a body is at rest, then its velocity-time graph is
questions.explanation: Velocity-time graph when the body is at rest: By saying that a body is a rest, it means that the velocity (v) of the body is zero at the time (t). So, the velocity-time graph of a body at rest is a straight line on the time axis.

questions.question: Which of the following is correct about the following distance-time graph?

options.html: speed of A is less than speed of B
speed of A is equal to speed of B
speed of A is greater than speed of B

questions.explanation: In the distance-time graph shown above, the speed of object A is greater than speed of object B as the object A is covering more distance per unit time.

questions.question: Four cars A, B, C and D are moving on a levelled road. Their distance-time graph is given as under. The correct statement is:

options.html: Car D is faster than Car C

questions.explanation: The slope of a distance time graph determines the speed of the body, so steeper the slope greater will be the speed of the body. From the above graph we can come to a conclusion that car C has the highest speed and car B has the least speed.

questions.question: If we get a curve line in the displacement-time graph then the velocity is:
questions.explanation: For non-uniform motion, the displacement-time graph of a body is a curved line. This curved line proves that its velocity is non-uniform.

questions.question: The third equation of motion is:
questions.explanation:
The third equation of motion draws a relation between final velocity, initial velocity, acceleration of the body, and its displacement.
The third equation of motion is v2 = u2 + 2aS
Here, v = final velocity, u = initial velocity, a = acceleration and S = displacement.

questions.question:
Match the following with the correct response.

(a) Rate of change of velocity (i) Uniform circular motion
(b) Rate of change of displacement (ii) Velocity
(c) Rate of change of distance (iii) Acceleration
(d) Rate of change of speed in a circular path (iv) Speed
questions.explanation:
Acceleration of a body is defined as the rate of change of its velocity with time.
The velocity of a body is defined as the rate of change of its displacement with time.
The speed of a body is the distance travelled by it per unit time.
When a body moves along a circular path, then its direction of motion keeps changing continuously. Therefore, the motion along a circular path is said to be accelerated.

questions.question:
Match the following with the correct response.

(a) Ist equation of motion (i) v2 = u2 + 2aS
(b) IInd equation of motion (ii) v = u + at
(c) IIIrd equation of motion (iii) {tex} - \left( {\frac{{v - u}}{t}} \right){/tex}
(d) Retardation (iv) S = ut +{tex}\frac 1 2{/tex}at2
questions.explanation:
1. Ist equation of motion is {tex}v = u + at{/tex}
    where  the initial velocity = u, final velocity = v, Time = t and Acceleration = a
  2. IInd equation of motion is, S = ut +{tex}\frac 1 2{/tex}at2
    Here Initial velocity of the object = u
    The final velocity of the object = v
    Acceleration = a
    Time = t
    Distance covered in given time = s
  3. IIIrd equation of motion is {tex}{v^2} = {u^2} + 2aS{/tex}
  4. Retardation: Negative acceleration is called retardation. If the velocity of a body is decreasing with respect to time, the acceleration is said to be negative. 
    Retardation = {tex} - \left( {\frac{{v - u}}{t}} \right){/tex}    .

questions.question:
Match the following with the correct response.

(a) Graph showing uniform velocity (i) Straight-line sloping upwards
(b) Graph showing uniformly accelerated velocity (ii) Curved line
(c) Graph showing uniformly retarded (iii) Straight-line sloping downwards
(d) Graph showing non-uniform motion (iv) Straight-line parallel to x-axis
questions.explanation:
1. The slope of a Velocity – time graph of an object moving in rectilinear motion with uniform velocity in a straight line and parallel to x-axis when velocity is taken along the y-axis and time is taken along the x-axis.
  2. Graph showing uniformly accelerated velocity then straight line sloping upwards.
  3. In the velocity-time graph of a body moving with uniform retardation is a straight line sloping downwards.
  4. If an object is travelling at a variable angular speed around a fixed axis or center point, then the motion of that object will be defined as the uniform circular motion. In this case, the object travels around a curved path and it will have some radial acceleration due to which its velocity would change direction every second.

questions.question:
Which of the following statement is correct?

  1. When a body starts from rest, u = 0
  2. When a body comes to rest, v = 0
  3. For a body under free fall, a = −9.8m/s2
  4. For uniform velocity, a = 0
questions.explanation:
A body starting from rest and when it comes to rest has velocity equal to zero.
A body moving with uniform velocity has zero acceleration since there is no change in velocity.
A body under free fall has an acceleration of 9.8 ms-2.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: The slope of distance-time graph gives instantaneous-
questions.explanation: The slope of a distance-time graph gives the instantaneous speed of the moving object. Instantaneous speed can be more or less than average speed.

questions.question: Distance is a ________ quantity.
questions.explanation: Those physical quantities that possess the only magnitude are called scalar quantities. Distance is a scalar quantity since the direction is not required to represent distance. It is the total length of the actual path covered by a body.

questions.question: A body can be at rest and in motion at the same time. The above statement is-
questions.explanation: An object which is at rest can also be in motion with respect to different reference points. For example, a passenger traveling in a bus. The passenger is at rest with respect to the fellow passengers but it is in motion with respect to the bus.

questions.question: A body is moving with a velocity of 10m/s. If the motion is uniform, what will be the velocity after 10 second?
questions.explanation: A body moving with uniform motion never changes its speed. So, the velocity of the body remains the same after 10 seconds that is 10m/s.

questions.question: Distance can never be ________ for a moving body.
questions.explanation: The actual length of the path travelled by a moving body in a given interval of time is called the distance travelled by the body. It is a scalar quantity and its value never be zero or negative.

questions.question: A car has a uniform acceleration of 8 ms-2. What distance will it cover in 20 seconds after start?
questions.explanation:
Distance covered (S) = ut + {tex}1\over2{/tex}at2.
Here, a = 8 m/s2, t = 20 s.
Therefore, S = 0 {tex}\times{/tex} 20 + {tex}1\over2{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 20 {tex}\times{/tex} 20 = 1600 m.

questions.question: A man walks at 1 m/s for 60 minutes. He takes rest for 20 minutes and then walks at a speed of 1 m/s for 60 minutes. Find his average speed.
questions.explanation:
Average speed = {tex}Total\ distance \ travelled\over Total\ time \ taken{/tex}
distance= 1 m/s {tex}\times{/tex} 3600 sec = 3600 m,
Average speed = {tex}(3600 +3600)m\over(3600 + 3600 +1200)sec{/tex} = {tex}7200\  m\over 8400 \ sec{/tex} = 0.86 m/s.
It can be also represented in km/h.
In km/h,the average speed of the man is 0.0516 km/h.

questions.question: The reference point for measuring the position of a body is called:
questions.explanation: The reference point for measuring the position of a body is called the origin. Every distance is measured with respect to the origin.

questions.question: Under what conditions are the magnitude of the average velocity of an object equal to its average speed?

options.html: When a body moves in a straight line with reversing its direction.
When a body is moving in uniform motion.
When a body moves in a straight line without reversing its direction.
When a body is moving at constant acceleration.

questions.explanation: When an object moves along a straight line in the same direction, its total path length is the magnitude of displacement. Hence its average speed is equal to the magnitude of average velocity.

questions.question: A train is travelling at a speed of 90 km/h. Brakes are applied so as to produce a uniform acceleration of -0.5 m/s2. Find how far will the train go before it is brought to rest.
questions.explanation:
Initial velocity (u) = 90 km/h = {tex}90\times\frac5{80}{/tex}  = 25 m/s,
a = -0.5m/s2
v = 0 m/s
From third equation of motion,
v2 - u2 = 2aS
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} S = {tex}\frac{v^2-\;u^2}{2a}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} S = {tex}\frac{(0)^2-\;(25)^2}{2\times(-0.5)}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} S = 625 m

questions.question: If a bus accelerates uniformly from rest to attain a speed of 144 km h-1 in 20 seconds it covers a distance of
questions.explanation:
Here, initial velocity (u) = 0 m/s, final velocity (v) = 144 km/h =144 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}5\over18{/tex} = 40 m/s, t = 20s, a = 40 - {tex}0\over20{/tex} = 2 m/s2 .
S= ut + {tex}1 \over 2{/tex}at2 = 0 {tex}\times{/tex} 20 + {tex}1 \over 2{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 20 {tex}\times{/tex} 20 = 400m.

questions.question: Odometer is a device installed in vehicles which measure:
questions.explanation: The speed of a running vehicle at any instant of time is shown by an instrument called 'speedometer'. The distance travelled by a vehicle is measured by another instrument called 'odometer' which is installed in the vehicle. It records the distance in kilometres.

questions.question: What is the ratio of the CGS unit to the SI unit of velocity?
questions.explanation:
CGS unit of velocity is cm/s and the SI unit of velocity is m/s.
The ratio of CGS unit to SI unit= cm/m
(1 meter is equal to 100 cm.)
Therefore, ratio = {tex}1\over100 {/tex}= 1: 100

questions.question: What is the ratio of distance to displacement?
questions.explanation: Distance is the actual length covered by a body and displacement is the shortest distance between the initial and final position. So, the distance will be equal to or more than displacement.

questions.question:
Match the following with the correct response.

(a) Non- uniform motion (i) The slope of the graph
(b) Speed from the distance-time graph (ii) {tex}\frac{{{\text{Initial velocity }} + {\text{Final velocity}}}}{2}{/tex}
(c) Distance covered from the speed-time graph (iii) The area under the speed-time graph
(d) Average velocity (iv) If a body covers unequal distances in equal intervals of time.
questions.explanation:
• Non-uniform motion - A body has a non-uniform motion if it travels unequal distances in equal intervals of time.
  • Speed from the distance-time graph- The distance-time graph of a body can be used to calculate the speed of the body. The slope of a distance-time graph indicates speed.
  • Distance covered from the speed-time graph - The distance traveled by a moving body in a given time can be calculated from its speed-time graph. The area enclosed by the speed-time graph with the X-axis gives the distance covered.
  • Average velocity - If the velocity of a body is always changing, but changing at a uniform rate, then the average velocity is given by the "arithmetic mean" of the initial and final velocity for a given period of time, that is Average velocity = {tex}\frac{{{\text{Initial velocity }} + {\text{Final velocity}}}}{2}{/tex}
