If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: P. E of your body is maximum when you
questions.explanation: The potential energy of the body depends on how far the object is from the centre of earth within the sphere of gravitational influence. Technically, Potential energy equals to the product of the weight of the object and its distance from the centre of the earth. If you stand up, your centre of gravity is brought up that will increase your distance from the centre of the earth (not significantly though). So the potential energy possessed by your body will be more if you’re standing than sitting on the ground or lying on the ground.

questions.question:
Statement A: An aeroplane flying at an altitude possesses only K. E.
Statement B: An aeroplane flying at an altitude possesses both K. E and P. E.
Which of the two statement is true?
questions.explanation: The areoplane has both kinetic energy and potential energy. When it is in flight it will fly above the ground so it is displaced by certain distance and hence it posses potential energy. As it is in motion it posses kinetic energy.

questions.question:
Find the incorrect statement

  1. The P. E of a freely falling object increases progressively.
  2. Body at rest may possess energy.
  3. There is no transfer of energy between you and the rock when you push a huge rack and fail to move it.
  4. K.E. is converted into heat and sound energy when a freely falling object eventually stops on reaching the ground.
questions.explanation: When an object starts to fall freely, the K.E. increase with a decrease in potential energy, and total mechanical energy remains constant. So the given statement is incorrect.

questions.question: When you compress a coil spring you do work on it. The elastic P. E energy
questions.explanation: The work done by you will be stored in the form of electric potential energy. So, when you compress a coil spring you do work on it then the elastic P. E energy increase.

questions.question: P.E of a spring increases when it is in the state of
questions.explanation: The potential energy of a spring increases when it is in the state of expansion and compression. Elastic potential energy increases with compression and gravitational potential energy increases on expansion.

questions.question: What happens to the kinetic energy of a freely falling object which eventually stops on reaching the ground?

options.html: It changes into heat and sound energy

questions.explanation:
When a freely falling body eventually stops on reaching the ground, its kinetic energy appears in the form of:

  1. heat (the body and the ground become warmer due to collision).
  2. sound (produced due to collision of the body with the ground).

questions.question: Two particles of masses 2g and 4g have equal momentum. What is the ratio between their kinetic energies?
questions.explanation:
Momentum is the product of mass and velocity.
P = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} u
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} u = {tex}\;\frac p2{/tex}.
Again p = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} v
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} v ={tex}\;\frac p4{/tex} 
So, velocity of first particle is twice the second.
Ratio of kinetic energy = {tex}\;\frac{\displaystyle\frac12\times2\times\frac p2\times\frac p2}{\displaystyle\frac12\times4\times\frac p4\times\frac p4}=\frac{\displaystyle\frac p4}{\displaystyle\frac p8}=\frac84=\frac21{/tex} = 2 : 1.

questions.question:
Statement A: Speed of a gas molecule decreases with increase in temperature.
Statement B: P. E of the body decreases as it falls towards the surface of the Earth.
Which of the two statements is true?

options.html: statement A and B are false

questions.explanation:
The average kinetic energy of a gas particle is directly proportional to the temperature. An increase in temperature increases the speed at which the gas molecules move. So statement  A is a false statement.
As the body falls from height h, its potential energy decreases while the kinetic energy begins to increase; the total mechanical energy at any point being the same i.e. m g h. So, statement B is a true statement.

questions.question:
Match the following with the correct response.

(a) K.E (i) mgh
(b) P.E (ii) P {tex}\times{/tex} t
(c) P (iii) {tex}\frac{w}{t}{/tex}
(d) E (iv) {tex}\frac{1}{2}m{v^2}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
• Kinetic energy is the energy of motion. KE = {tex}\;\frac12{/tex} mv2
  • The energy possessed by an object because of its position is called potential energy. P.E. = mgh
  • The rate of doing work is called power. P = {tex}w \over t{/tex}
  • Energy = Power {tex}\times{/tex} time , E = P {tex}\times{/tex} t

questions.question:
Match the following with the correct response.

(a) Bullet fired from a gun (i) Potential energy changes to kinetic energy
(b) A flying bird (ii) Kinetic energy
(c) In a falling stone (iii) Potential energy
(d) Water at dam (iv) Kinetic & Potential energy
questions.explanation:
• Bullet fired from guns is kinetic energy because Kinetic energy is the energy of motion.
  • A flying bird depends on motion and position also so its example of optional and kinetic energy.
  • In a falling stone potential energy changes to kinetic energy.
  • Water at dam stored in a particular position so, it’s an example of potential energy.

questions.question:
When an object starts to fall freely, which statement is incorrect about it

  1. Its potential energy is increased and kinetic energy is decreased
  2. Its kinetic energy is increased and potential energy is decreased
  3. Potential energy remains unchanged
  4. Kinetic energy remains unchanged
questions.explanation: When an object starts to fall freely, the K.E. increase with a decrease in potential energy, and total mechanical energy remains constant.  So, statements A, C, and D are incorrect and B is correct.

questions.question:
An object is falling from a height h when it has fallen a height {tex}\;\frac h2{/tex}, it will possess

  1. Half kinetic energy
  2. Half potential energy
  3. Only kinetic energy
  4. Only potential energy
questions.explanation:
At a height {tex}\;\frac h2{/tex} PE = mg ({tex}\;\frac h2{/tex}) = {tex}\;\frac12{/tex} mgh
The rest ({tex}\frac12{/tex} mgh) of the PE is converted into K.E.
Thus, at a height ({tex}\frac h2{/tex}), PE = KE = {tex}\;\frac12{/tex} mgh

questions.question: Our hands become warm when rubbed against each other. This is because:

options.html: Chemical energy is converted into heat energy
Mechanical energy is converted into heat energy
Potential energy is converted into heat energy
K. E is converted into heat energy

questions.explanation: When we rub our hands against each other they become warm. Here, heat is generated due to the loss of kinetic energy of the hands due to friction. Thus, heat is a form of energy that can be produced when some form of energy is lost. In this example, kinetic energy is converted into heat energy.

questions.question: The head of a nail hammered into the wooden plank becomes hot. This is because of:

options.html: conversion of chemical energy into heat energy
conversion of P. E into heat energy
conversion of mechanical energy into heat energy
conversion of K. E into heat energy

questions.explanation: The head of a nail hammered into the wooden plank by speed the K.E. is converted into heat energy. So, the head of a nail hammered into the wooden plank becomes hot.

questions.question: “Lever transforms mechanical energy into muscular energy” the above statement is:
questions.explanation: The lever does not create energy. Lever converts a small force applied over a long distance to a large force applied over a small distance. Work is the product of force and the distance, W = Fd. So the total work done is the same with or without the lever. Muscular energy is present in the muscle of animals. Lever transforms mechanical energy into muscular energy is a false statement.

tests.instructionHtml:

General Instructions:

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: According to the law of conservation of energy:

options.html: Energy can be transformed from one form to another

questions.explanation: According to the law of conservation of energy, energy neither be created nor be destroyed, it can be transformed from one form to another. The total energy of the universe always remains constant.

questions.question: A ball is dropped from a height h. The velocity of the ball when it reaches the ground is 10 m/s. Find h (g = 10 ms-2).
questions.explanation:
Initial velocity (u) = 0,
Final velocity (v) = 10 m/s,
v2 - u2 = 2gh
(10)2 – (0)2 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 {tex}\times{/tex} h
100 = 20 h
h = {tex}\frac{100}{20}{/tex} 
h = 5m.

questions.question:
Statement A: Mechanical energy of a system is always conserved.
Statement B: The total energy of the system is always conserved.
Which of the two statements is true?
questions.explanation:
1. Mechanical energy is not always conserved. Mechanical energy is the sum of kinetic and potential energy in a system. Mechanical energy is conserved so long as we ignore air resistance, friction, etc. When we don't ignore outside forces, such as those just mentioned, mechanical energy is not conserved.
  2. According to the law of conservation of energy the total energy of the system is always conserved.
    So, Statement B is true stamen and Statement A is a false statement.

questions.question:
Match the following with the correct response.

(a) Battery (i) Electrical energy converts into light energy
(b) Bulb (ii) Sound energy converts into electrical energy
(c) Steam engine (iii) Steam energy converts into mechanical energy
(d) Microphone (iv) Chemical energy converts into electrical energy
questions.explanation:
• In Battery, chemical energy converts into electrical energy.
  • In Bulb, electrical energy converts into light energy.
  • In the Steam engine, steam energy converts into mechanical energy.
  • In microphones,  sound energy converts into electrical energy.

questions.question: Work done by the gravitational force of earth on a satellite along a circular path is
questions.explanation:
When a satellite moves around the Earth along a circular path, then the force of gravity acts on it towards the centre. The force of gravity of the earth and the direction of movement of the satellite are perpendicular to each other means {tex}\theta{/tex} = 90°.
Therefore W = F {tex}\times{/tex} S cos {tex}\theta{/tex}. (cos 90° = 0)
 W = 0 J.

questions.question: The linear momentum of a body of mass 2 kg is 16 kg ms-1. What is its K. E?
questions.explanation:
Momentum = mass {tex}\times{/tex} velocity
Velocity = {tex}\;\frac{momentum\;}{mass}{/tex}= {tex}\;\frac{16}2{/tex} = 8m/s.
Kinetic energy = {tex}\;\frac12{/tex} mv2.
K.E ={tex}\;\frac12{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 82 = 64 J.

questions.question: The amount of work done when a force of one dyne acting in an object displaces it through a distance of one centimeter along its own direction is known as
questions.explanation: The basic unit of energy or work in the CGS system, equal to the amount of work done by a force of one dyne acting through a distance of one centimeter.

questions.question: Which provides more energy to human body after digestion?
questions.explanation: Calorific value of butter is higher than potato, rice and salad. So, one kg of butter provides more energy to human body after digestion in comparison to 1 kg of rice, potatoes and salad.

questions.question:
Statement A: If two bodies have the same K. E, then their momentum will also be equal
Statement B: Work done by the centripetal force is always greater than zero.
Which of the following statement is true?

options.html: statement A is true, B is false
both the statement A and B are true
statement B is true, A is false
neither statement A nor statement B is true.

questions.explanation:
1. Two bodies having equal kinetic energy may or may not have equal momentum.
  2. The work done by a centripetal force is always zero.

So, statements A and B both are not true statements.

questions.question:
Match the following with correct response.

Column A Column B
(1) Kinetic energy (A) Energy due to position
(2) Potential energy (B) The rate of doing work
(3) Work (C) Force displaces an object in the direction of force
(4) Power (D) Energy due to motion
questions.explanation:
1. Kinetic energy  is the energy of motion. KE = {tex}\;\frac12{/tex} mv2
  2. Energy possessed by an object because of its position is called potential energy.
  3. Work is defined as a force acting upon an object to cause a displacement .
  4. Rate of doing work is called power.

questions.question:
Match the following with correct response.

Column A Column B
(1) Velocity effects (A) Energy
(2) Maximum work done (B) {tex}90^\circ {/tex}
(3) Work done is zero (C) {tex}O^\circ {/tex}
(4) Work done, when force act obliquely (D) {tex}F \times S \times \cos \theta {/tex}
questions.explanation:
1. As the velocity of object increases it's Kinetic energy also increases.
  2. W= F x S cos θ ( cos 00 = 1)  So, w = F x S means maximum work is done.
  3. W= F x S cos θ ( cos 900 = 0)  So, w = 0 means zero work is done.
  4. W= F x S cos θ where F = force, d = displacement

questions.question:
Match the following with correct response.

Column A Column B
(1) Law of conservation of energy (A) kinetic energy
(2) Stretched rubber band possess (B) Energy neither be created nor be destroyed
(3) At mean position pendulum attains maximum (C) Interconvertible
(4) Various energy forms are (D) Potential energy
questions.explanation:
1. Law of conservation of energy is stated as energy neither be created nor be destroyed but it converted into one form to another form.
  2. Stretched rubber band possess potential energy.
  3. At mean position pendulum attains maximum kinetic energy.
  4. Various energy forms are Inter convertible.

questions.question:
Match the following with the correct response.

(a) Nm is also called (i) Potential energy is equal to kinetic energy
(b) Freefall (ii) Work was done
(c) A donkey is working with a load on its back (iii) No work is done
(d) An engine is pulling a train (iv) Erg
questions.explanation:
• Nm is also called Erg.
  • In free fall potential energy is equal to kinetic energy.
  • A donkey is carrying a load on its back, the force of gravity on the load is in the downward direction, whereas the displacement is in the horizontal direction, i.e., the force and the displacement are perpendicular to each other. There is no displacement in the direction of the force of gravity, and therefore, work done is zero.
  • An engine pulling a train on a railway line is said to be doing work.

questions.question:
Which of the following statement are incorrect

  1. A bird sitting on a tree possesses potential energy only.
  2. A stationary object may have energy.
  3. A flying bird has kinetic energy only.
  4. An aeroplane running on the run-way possesses kinetic & potential energy both.
questions.explanation:
1. A bird sitting on a tree possesses potential energy only is a correct statement.
  2. A stationary object may have energy is a correct statement.
  3. A flying bird has kinetic energy only this is the incorrect statement because A flying bird has kinetic energy as well as positional energy also.
  4. An aeroplane running on the run-way possesses kinetic energy only. So the given statement is incorrect.

questions.question:
Which of the following is not a vector quantity

  1. Energy
  2. Kinetic energy
  3. Work done
  4. Power

options.html: A, B and C are correct
All A, B, C, and D are correct

questions.explanation:
1. Energy is a scalar. This is because energy is defined as the scalar product of the force (F) and displacement (S).
  2. Kinetic energy is a scalar because it is defined to be a scalar.
  3. Work is a scalar quantity, i.e. work has only magnitude but no direction.
  4. Power = work done per unit time. Since work is a scalar quantity, power is also a scalar quantity.

So, Energy, Kinetic energy, Work done, and Power all are scalar quantities.

tests.name: Sound Test-01
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Sound Test-01 for CBSE class 09 Science. Students are instructed o read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Crests and troughs are formed in:-
questions.explanation: In a transverse wave, the medium has particles that vibrate in a direction perpendicular to the direction of the propagation of waves. Here the formation of crest and trough takes place. The polarization of the transverse wave is possible. The upward movement from the mean position is called crest and downward movement is called the trough.

questions.question: X – rays, light rays are electromagnetic waves is a
questions.explanation: X-rays are a form of electromagnetic radiation, as are radio waves, infrared radiation, visible light, ultraviolet radiation, and microwaves. These waves do not require a material medium for its propagation.

questions.question: Sound is a:-

options.html: Neither longitudinal wave not transverse wave
Both longitudinal wave and transverse wave

questions.explanation: The wave formed because of the oscillation; parallel to the disturbance is called a longitudinal wave. Sound is a longitudinal wave as the wave particles of sound travel parallel to the direction of motion by forming compression and rarefaction.

questions.question: The maximum displacement of the particles of a medium from their mean position is called the
questions.explanation: The maximum displacement of the particles of a medium from their mean position during the propagation of a wave is called the amplitude of the wave. The loudness of sound depends upon amplitude.

questions.question: Which wave property determines loudness?
questions.explanation: The loudness of a sound depends upon amplitude, the more the amplitude of a wave, the more is the loudness produced. Amplitude is the maximum displacement of a particle from its mean position.

questions.question: A source produces 20 crests and 20 troughs in 0.2 seconds. Find the frequency of the wave.
questions.explanation:
One crest and a trough make a single wavelength.
Therefore, according to the question,20 wavelengths are made.
Therefore, time = 0.2 sec
Time period=0.2/20 = 0.01 second.
Frequency = 1/time period = 1/0.01 = 100 Hz.

questions.question:
“Wave motion shifts mass from one region to another”
The above statement is
questions.explanation: Wave motion shifts wave energy from one region to another region. Mass is not transferred during wave motion.

questions.question: Calculate the wavelength of a sound wave whose frequency is 820 Hz and speed is 440 ms-1 in a given medium.
questions.explanation:
v= 440 ms-1 ; frequency = 840 Hz

Wavelength= {tex}\frac{wave\;speed\;}{frequency}\;=\frac{440}{840}{/tex} = 0.5 m.

questions.question: A boat at anchor is rocked by waves whose consecutive crests are 100m apart. The wave velocity of the moving crests is 20 ms-1. What is the frequency of rocking the boat?
questions.explanation:
Distance between two consecutive crests is called the wavelength.
Therefore, wavelength= 100 m.
Here,wave velocity = 20 ms-1 
frequency = wave velocity/ wavelength.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} frequency= 20/100 = 0.2 Hz.

questions.question:
Find the incorrect statement.

  1. The speed of the wave doesn’t depend on properties of the medium.
  2. Ultrasound is produced by electronic oscillator.
  3. 120 dB means intensity is 1 W/m2.
  4. The distance between crest and trough is {tex}\frac\lambda2{/tex}.
questions.explanation: The speed of a sound wave depends on the properties of the medium through which it moves and the only way to change the speed is to change the properties of the medium. So, given statement is incorrect statement.

questions.question: In which of the following medium, speed of sound is maximum?
questions.explanation: The speed of sound is faster in a solid medium in comparison to liquid and gaseous medium. Aluminium is solid and metal, so the speed of sound will be maximum in it. The speed of sound in Aluminium at 25oC is 6420 ms-1.

questions.question: Hertz is a unit of ________.
questions.explanation: Hertz (Hz) is the SI unit of frequency. Frequency is the number of vibration per second.

questions.question:
Match the following with the correct response.

(1) A moving disturbance (A) Mechanical waves
(2) Propagation require material medium (B) Oscillatory motion
(3) Particles vibrates perpendicular to the (C) Transverse waves direction of wave propagation
(4) Back and forth motion (D) Wave
questions.explanation:
• A wave can be described as a disturbance that travels through a medium from one location to another location. 
  • Mechanical Waves are waves that propagate through a material medium (solid, liquid, or gas) at a wave speed which depends on the elastic and inertial properties of that medium.
  • A transverse wave is a moving wave that consists of oscillations occurring perpendicular (right-angled) to the direction of energy transfer (or the propagation of the wave). If a transverse wave is moving in the positive x-direction, its oscillations are in up and down directions that lie in the y–z plane.
  • Oscillatory motion can be termed as the repeated motion in which an object repeats the same movement over and over,i.e., to and fro or back and forth motion.

questions.question:
Longitudinal waves are produced in-

  1. Solids and liquids
  2. Gases
  3. In solids and liquids only
  4. In solids and gases only
questions.explanation: A longitudinal wave is a wave in which particles of the medium move in a direction parallel to the direction that the wave moves. The longitudinal wave needs a material medium to travel, i.e, solids, liquids, and gases.

questions.question:
Which of the following does not require medium for propagation?

  1. Sound waves
  2. Light waves
  3. Radio waves
  4. Water waves
questions.explanation: Light and Radio waves are electromagnetic waves that do not require any medium of propagation.

tests.name: Sound Test-02
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Sound Test-02 for CBSE class 09 Science. Students are instructed o read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question:
Statement A: Speed of sound is same at different temperature
Statement B: Loudness is measured in decibels.
Which of the two statements is true?
questions.explanation:
Speed of sound is different at different temperature. With increase in temperature, speed of sound increases.

The loudness of sound is measured in units called decibels (dB). A decibel unit expresses the relative intensity of sounds on a scale from zero for the average least perceptible sound to about 100 dB, which is near the level most people find uncomfortably loud.

questions.question: Frequency of infrasonic waves is ________ than the frequency of ultrasound waves.
questions.explanation:
The frequency of infrasonic waves is less than the frequency of ultrasound waves.
The frequency of infrasonic waves is less than 20Hz whereas the frequency of ultrasonic waves are greater than 20,000 Hz.

questions.question: The velocity, wavelength and time period of a wave are related as:-
questions.explanation:
Distance covered by sound wave in unit time is called the velocity of the sound wave.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Velocity= {tex}\frac{Distance}{Time\;taken}{/tex}
If distance = {tex}\lambda{/tex} and Time = T
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Velocity= {tex} \frac{\lambda }{T}{/tex}
{tex}V = \frac{\lambda }{T}{/tex}

questions.question: The upper limit of frequency of sound waves audible to human beings is
questions.explanation: The sound whose frequency lies between 20 Hz and 20000 Hz which human beings are able to hear is called audible sound. So, the audible range of the human ear is 20-20000 Hz.

questions.question: The distance between a consecutive crest and trough is x. The wavelength of the wave is
questions.explanation: Wavelength is the distance between two consecutive crests or trough in case of a transverse wave. Here, the distance between the consecutive crest and trough is x, so, the wavelength will be 2x.

questions.question: Children can hear sound of frequency up to
questions.explanation: Children under the age of five can hear sound up to 25,000Hz which is more than normal adult hearing range. As people grow older their ears become less sensitive to a higher frequency.

questions.question: A wave moves a distance of 10 m in 0.05 second. Find the wavelength of the wave if frequency is 200 Hz?
questions.explanation:
We know that, velocity = {tex}\frac{\text{distance travelled}}{\text{time taken }}{/tex}
= 200m/s.
Now,
Wavelength = {tex}\frac{\text{wave speed}}{\text{frequency}}{/tex}
 {tex}\Rightarrow \frac{200{\text{ }}m{s^{ - 1}}}{200\,Hz}{/tex} = 1m.
Therefore, the wavelength of the wave is 1 m.

questions.question:
Statement A: Speeds greater than the speed of sound in the air are called infrasonic speed.
Statement B: The amount of sound energy, passing each second through unit area is called intensity of sound.
Which of the above two statements is true?
questions.explanation:
The speed that is greater than the speed of sound is known as supersonic.
The amount of sound energy, passing each second through unit area is called intensity of sound.
So, statement B is a true statement and statement A is a false statement.

questions.question: The frequency of a source of sound is 546 Hz. It vibrates _________ times in a second.
questions.explanation:
Frequency is defined as the number of vibrations or waves produced per second.
If a source of sound has a frequency of 546 Hz, it means that it vibrates 546 times in one second.

questions.question: A sound wave travels at a speed of 344 ms-1. If its wavelength is 0.02 m, what is the frequency of the wave?
questions.explanation:
Frequency of wave = {tex}\frac{\text{wave speed}}{\text{wavelength}}{/tex}
Frequency = {tex}\frac{344m{s^{ - 1}}}{0.02m}{/tex} = 17200 Hz

questions.question: Which of the following condition is not favourable for producing higher pitch?

options.html: Length of the vibrating portion should be small.
Vibrating string should be very thin.
Length of the vibrating column should be shorter.

questions.explanation: The sound of high pitch cannot be produced by vibrating string of thicker diameter. This is because thick strings vibrate with lower frequency. For example, thin strings of a guitar or sitar give out the sound of the higher pitch as they vibrate with a greater frequency than the thick strings.

questions.question: The frequency of sound is 100 Hz. How many times does it vibrate in a minute?
questions.explanation:
The frequency of sound is the number of vibration per second.
Here, frequency = 100Hz. One minute = 60 seconds.
So, the number of vibrations in one minute = 60 {tex} \times{/tex} 100 = 6000 Hz.

questions.question: What will be the wavelength of a sound wave whose frequency is 220 Hz and speed is 440 ms-1 in a given medium?
questions.explanation:
Frequency = 220Hz

Wave speed = 440 ms-1.

Since, wavelength= wave speed / frequency

=>Wavelength = 440/220 = 2 m.

questions.question:
Statement A: Sound travels faster in summer season than in winter season
Statement B: The speed of sound does not depend on the pressure of the medium. If the temperature of the medium remains constant
Which of the two statements is true?
questions.explanation:
The speed of sound increases with temperature. So, sound travels faster in summer than in the winter season.
At a constant temperature, the gas pressure has no effect on the speed of sound, since the density will increase, and since pressure and density (also proportional to pressure) have equal but opposite effects on the speed of sound, and the two contributions cancel out exactly.

questions.question:
Match the following with the correct response.

(1) Amplitude (A) Duration to complete one oscillation
(2) Time period (B) Distance covered by sound in unit time
(3) Frequency (C) Number of oscillation in one second
(4) Velocity (D) Maximum displacement from mean position
questions.explanation:
• The maximum displacement of the particles of a medium from their mean positions during the propagation of a wave is called the amplitude of the wave.
  • The time required to produce one complete wave (or wave cycle) is called the time period of the wave.
  • The frequency of a wave is defined as the number of waves produced per second.
  • The distance travelled by a wave in one second is called the velocity of the wave.

tests.instructionHtml:

  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question:
Match the following with correct response.

(1) Speed of sound in Aluminium. (A) 326 ms-1
(2) Speed of sound in water. (B) 1484 ms-1
(3) Speed of sound in air. (C) 6420 ms-1
(4) Speed of sound in oxygen gas. (D) 343 ms-1
questions.explanation:
The speed of sound is not always the same. Speed of sound in different for different mediums.

  1. Speed of sound in Aluminium is  6420ms-1.
  2. Speed of sound in water is 1484ms-1.
  3. Speed of sound in air  is 343ms-1.
  4. Speed of sound in oxygen gas 326ms-1.

questions.question:
Which of the following is correct?

  1. {tex}V = \frac{\lambda }{t}{/tex}
  2. {tex}V = \lambda T{/tex}
  3. {tex}V = v\lambda {/tex}
  4. {tex}v = \frac{1}{T}{/tex}
questions.explanation:
Frequency = {tex}\frac {1}{T}{/tex}
Wave velocity, V = Distance travelled by a {tex}\frac{wave}{Time\;taken}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow V = \frac {\lambda}{T}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow V = \frac {1}{T} \times \lambda{/tex}
Therefore, V= v {tex}\times \lambda{/tex}        [Since, v ={tex}\frac {1}{T}{/tex}]
Wave velocity = Frequency {tex}\times{/tex} Wavelength
So, (A), (C) and (D) are correct

questions.question:
“Due to the high power of ultrasound waves. It is used for breaking and cutting objects”
The above statement is
questions.explanation: Ultrasound waves vibrate at frequency higher than 20,000Hz. Ultrasonic waves are directed towards kidneys.These waves with such a high vibration break the stones into tiny pieces so they can pass out of the kidney with the urine.

questions.question:
Statement A: Human beings are ultrasound deaf
Statement B: Ultrasonic waves can travel easily in solid, liquid, and gases.
Which of the two statements is true?
questions.explanation:
• Human beings are ultrasound deaf as humans are not able to hear the frequency of sound above 20,000Hz.
  • Ultrasound travels through various media including gases, liquids, and solids, but cannot travel through a vacuum. The speed of sound varies by the medium it travels through. Sound is likely to travel faster through solids, followed by liquids and gases.

questions.question:
Match the following with the correct response.

(1) Laws of reflection of sound (A) Megaphone
(2) An application of multiple reflections of sound (B) Measures Depth of the sea
(3) Ultrasound (C) To monitor foetus inside the mother’s womb
(4) SONAR (D) Angle of reflection is equal to angle of incidence
questions.explanation:
• According to the laws of reflection of sound, the angle of incidence is equal to the angle of reflection of sound.
  • When a person speaks into the narrow end of the megaphone tube, the sound waves produced by his voice are prevented from spreading by successive reflections from the wider end of the megaphone tube.
  • Ultra-sonography uses ultrasounds for the examination of the foetus during pregnancy. The technique is used to monitor the growth, development, and well being of the foetus.
  • SONAR (Sound Navigation and Ranging): Sonar is a device which is used to find the depth of a sea, or to locate underwater objects like enemy's submarine, sunken ships, a shoal of fish, etc.

questions.question:
Ultrasound is produced by

  1. Tortoise
  2. Bats
  3. Dolphins
  4. Dogs
questions.explanation: Animals like dolphins, bats, and tortoises produce sounds of frequency higher than 20000 Hz known as ultrasonic sounds.

questions.question: A bat hears the echo of its squeak after 0.1 second. How far is the obstacle from the bat? Speed of sound is 344 ms-1
questions.explanation:
Speed of sound = 344ms-1.
Time taken to hear echo = 0.1s
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} distance=speed {tex}\times{/tex} time.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2d=344 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} d = 34.4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}d= 17.2
Therefore, the bat is 17.2 m from its obstacle.

questions.question: Sonar stands for ______.
questions.explanation: Sonar stands for Sound navigation and ranging. Sonar is a device that is used to find the depth of the sea or to locate underwater objects like the enemy's submarine, sunken ships, a shoal of fish, etc.

questions.question: The multiple reflection of sound in a hall is called
questions.explanation: When a number of echoes of the original sound are heard, each echo being fainter than the preceding one, such multiple echoes are called reverberations. In halls and auditorium, reverberation is reduced by using sound-absorbing substances. Sound absorbing panels are kept near the stage.

questions.question:
Statement A: Porpoises use ultrasound for navigation and location of food in dark
Statement B: When you listen to music or watch TV at home, the noise level is 100-120 decibels.
Which of the two statements is true?
questions.explanation:
Bat and porpoises use ultrasound for navigation and location of food in the dark. They contain ultrasound producing organs and receiver that help them to judge the position and location of food and predator.
Music and TV sounds that we hear have a noise level below 90 decibels.

questions.question: ‘The angle of incidence is equal to the angle of reflection’ is one of the law of reflection of
questions.explanation:
Like light, sound waves also get reflected at the surface of a solid or liquid and follow the laws of reflection.
 Laws of reflection: (a) Angle of incidence is equal to the angle of reflection. (b) The incident ray, the reflected ray and normal at the point of incidence all lie in the same plane.

questions.question: The good directional property of ultrasound waves are used for:-
questions.explanation: The good directional property of ultrasound waves are used for scanning objects and is used in the detection of cracks.

questions.question: A person clapped his hands near a cliff and heard the echo after 5 sec. What is the distance of the cliff from the person, if the speed of the sound V is taken as 346 ms-1?
questions.explanation:
Speed of sound = 346 ms-1 
Time taken = 5 sec.
Since,
distance = speed {tex}\times{/tex} time
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2d = 346 {tex}\times{/tex} 5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2d = 1730
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} d = 865 m

questions.question:
Which of the following statement is true?

  1. The frequency of man’s voice more than that of woman’s voice.
  2. Repeated claps of thunder in quick succession are called rolling of thunder.
  3. Timbre is a typical feature of a sound which enables us to distinguish between the sounds of same loudness and pitch.
questions.explanation:
Rolling thunder is repeated claps of thunder in quick succession. The sound of the same loudness and pitch produced by two different sources can be distinguished by timbre. Female's voice has more frequency components compared to men. Women speak at one octave higher than men.
So, statement (A) is incorrect and (B) and (C) are correct statements.

questions.question: During a thunderstorm, one observes lightning first and then hears the thunder, though both occur simultaneously. This is because
questions.explanation: During a thunderstorm, we observe lightning first and hears the sound of thunder later, although both occur simultaneously because light travels much faster than sound. The speed of sound through air is about 340 ms-1. The speed of light in air is about 300 million meters per second. Therefore, light travels faster than sound.

This is Sound Test-04 for CBSE class 09 Science. Students are instructed o read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Principle of reflection of sound is used in:-
questions.explanation:
Stethoscope is used to hear the sounds of internal organs of a patient; for diagnostic purposes. It works on the laws of reflection of sound.
Loudspeaker, Megaphone and bulb horn are devices used to send the sound in desired direction without spreading the sound all around. These devices act on the laws of reflection of sound wave.
Sound board is used to send the sound towards audience in big hall or auditorium. This works on the basis of laws of reflection of sound waves.

questions.question:
Match the following with the correct response.

(1) Echo (A) Bouncing back of sound
(2) Reverberation (B) Sound Navigation & Ranging
(3) SONAR (C) Persistence of sound due to repeated reflection
(4) Sound board

(D) Concave surface
questions.explanation:
• An echo is the repetition of the original sound heard after the sound is reflected from a distant, dense, and rigid object.
  • When a number of echoes of the original sound are heard, each echo being fainter than the preceding one, such multiple echoes are called reverberations.
  • SONAR (Sound Navigation and Ranging): Sonar is a device which is used to find the depth of the sea, or to locate underwater objects like enemy's submarine, sunken ships, shoal of fish, etc.
  • A soundboard is a concave board (curved board) that is placed behind the speaker. The speaker is made to stand at the focus of the concave soundboard. This setup solves the problem of interference of the sound waves with the large walls of the hall or auditorium.

questions.question:
Match the following with correct response.

(1) Audible range (A) Less than 20 Hz
(2) Infrasonic (B) Speed greater than sound in air
(3) Ultrasonic (C) More than 20,000 Hz
(4) Supersonic (D) 20 Hz to 20,000 Hz
questions.explanation:
The sound whose frequency lies between 20 Hz and 20000 Hz which we are able to hear is called audible sound.

Inaudible sounds having frequency less than 20 Hz are known as infrasonic sounds.

Inaudible sounds having frequecy more than 20000 Hz are known as ultrasonic sounds.

When a body is travelling at a speed greater than the speed of sound,we say that the body is travelling at a supersonic speed.

questions.question: Which part of human ear converts sound vibrations into electrical signals
questions.explanation: Cochlea

questions.question: The eardrum is a
questions.explanation: The eardrum is a stretched membranous structure at the junction of the outer ear and middle ear. The vibration of eardrum causes the production of sound that we hear. It is also known as the tympanic membrane.

questions.question: Cochlea, semicircular canals and the auditory nerve are associated with
questions.explanation: Cochlea, semicircular canals and auditory nerve are associated with inner ear. The internal ear contains colchea. The vibrating bones pass their vibrations to the colchea. The sensitive hair cells present in the colchea respond to the sound vibrations recieved and change them into nerve impulses. These impulses are then carried by the auditory nerves to the brain.

questions.question: Wave motion is a periodic ________ produced by a vibrating body.
questions.explanation: Wave motion is a periodic disturbance in the medium produced by a vibrating body. These disturbance moves into medium to produce sounds. Wave motion is also known as the propagation of disturbances.

questions.question:
Statement A: Molecules also move from one place to another in a wave motion.
Statement B: Electromagnetic waves don’t need a material medium to travel.
Which of the two statements is/are true?
questions.explanation:
Wave motion is an activity that carries energy from one place to another without actually moving any matter. In wave motion, energy-the ability to perform work, or to exert force over distance-is transmitted from one place to another without actually moving any matter along the wave. In some types of waves, such as those on the ocean, it might seem as though matter itself has been displaced; that is, it appears that the water has actually moved from its original position. In fact, this is not the case: molecules of water in an ocean wave move up and down, but they do not actually travel with the wave itself. Only the energy is moved.
Electromagnetic waves differ from mechanical waves in that they do not require a medium to propagate. This means that electromagnetic waves can travel not only through the air and solid materials but also through the vacuum of space.

questions.question:
Statement A: Molecules also move from one place to another in a wave motion.
Statement B: Electromagnetic waves don’t need a material medium to travel.
Which of the two statements is/are true?
questions.explanation:
Wave motion is an activity that carries energy from one place to another without actually moving any matter. In wave motion, energy-the ability to perform work, or to exert force over distance-is transmitted from one place to another without actually moving any matter along the wave. In some types of waves, such as those on the ocean, it might seem as though matter itself has been displaced; that is, it appears that the water has actually moved from its original position. In fact, this is not the case: molecules of water in an ocean wave move up and down, but they do not actually travel with the wave itself. Only the energy is moved.
Electromagnetic waves differ from mechanical waves in that they do not require a medium to propagate. This means that electromagnetic waves can travel not only through the air and solid materials but also through the vacuum of space.

questions.question:
“In a human ear, the sound is amplified in the outer ear as well as in the middle ear because of which we are able to listen loud sound only".
The above statement is
questions.explanation: In the human ear, the sound is amplified in the middle ear by three bones connected with the tympanic membrane. The amplified vibration is transmitted to the inner ear and is used to produce the sensation of hearing.

questions.question:
Statement A: An object moving at a speed greater than that of a sound is moving at ultrasonic speed.
Statement B: In wave motion, only energy is transferred.
Which of the two statements is true?
questions.explanation:
An object moving at a speed greater than that of sound is moving with supersonic speed and not ultrasonic speed.
Wave motion transfers energy from one point to another, which displace particles of the transmission medium–that is, with little or no associated mass transport. Waves consist, instead, of oscillations or vibrations (of a physical quantity), around almost fixed locations.

questions.question:
Match the following with the correct response.

(1) Crest (A) Depression
(2) Trough (B) Particles of the medium are farther
(3) Compression (C) Particles of the medium are closer
(4) Refraction (D) Elevation
questions.explanation:
• The crest is the point of maximum positive displacement (elevation) of a transverse wave.
  • The trough is the point of maximum negative displacement (depression) of a transverse wave.
  • Compression is that part of a longitudinal wave in which the particles of the medium are closer to one another than they normally are and there is a momentary decrease in the volume of the medium.
  • A rarefaction is that part of a longitudinal wave in which the particles of the medium are farther apart than that in the normal position and there is a momentary increase in the volume of the medium.

questions.question:
Match the following with the correct response.

(1) Loudness (A) Frequency
(2) Supersonic speed (B) Amplitude
(3) Pitch (C) Distinguish two sounds
(4) Timbre (D) Waveform
questions.explanation:
• It has been found that the greater the amplitude of the vibration of the source, the greater is the intensity (and hence, loudness) of sound and vice versa.
  • Speed greater than the speed of sound is called supersonic speed.
  • The Pitch of a sound depends upon the frequency (or wavelength) of vibration.
  • Timbre is the characteristic of a musical sound that enables us to distinguish between two sounds of the same pitch and loudness produced by two different sources. It depends upon the waveform of the sound.

questions.question:
Match the following with the correct response.

(1) Sound waves (A) Longitudinal waves
(2) Radio waves (B) Shock waves
(3) Supersonic jet (C) Transverse waves
(4) Mechanical waves (D) Elastic waves
questions.explanation:
• Sound waves in air (and any fluid medium) are longitudinal waves because particles of the medium through which the sound is transported vibrate parallel to the direction that the sound wave moves.
  • Waves that, like sound, vibrate in the same direction as their direction of propagation are longitudinal waves, while waves that vibrate at right angles to their direction of propagation are transverse waves. Radio waves are one kind of transverse wave.
  • An airplane or a supersonic jet moving at supersonic speed produces shock waves in air. These shock waves carry a large amount of energy. 
  • A mechanical wave is a wave that is an oscillation of matter and therefore transfers energy through a medium. Any kind of wave (mechanical or electromagnetic) has a certain energy. Mechanical waves can be produced only in media that possess elasticity and inertia.

questions.question:
Which of the following is incorrect-

  1. SI Unit of frequency is hertz
  2. SI Unit of wavelength is ms-1
  3. SI Unit of velocity is ms-2
  4. SI unit of time period is sec
questions.explanation: SI unit of wavelength is the metre (m) and velocity is ms-1.

chapters.name: Why Do We Fall ill (FA Only)

tests.name: Why Do We Fall Ill Test-01
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Why Do We Fall Ill Test-01 for CBSE class 09 Science. Students are instructed o read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.

  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Name of the biologist who established that pathogen is a disease agent.
questions.explanation:
Robert Koch was the first pathologist who established that diseases are mainly caused by pathogens. Disease-causing microbes are called pathogens.
Edward Jenner is credited with the discovery of vaccination. Robert Brown discovered the nucleus. Rudolf Virchow made contributions to the cell theory.

questions.question:
Find the incorrect statement:

  1. Proteins give more energy than that given by fats or carbohydrates.
  2. Germinated pulses contain more vitamins.
  3. A vegetarian diet is far better than a non-vegetarian diet.
  4. Fast food should always be preferred to a balanced diet.
questions.explanation: Proteins give less energy than given by fats and carbohydrates. Germinated pulses contain more vitamins. A vegetarian diet is better than a non-vegetarian diet. We should always prefer a balanced diet in place of fast food.

questions.question: Iodine test is used to detect:
questions.explanation: Iodine test is used to detect carbohydrates in food items. Food items containing carbohydrates like potatoes give a blue colour in the presence of iodine.

questions.question: Name the vitamin abundantly present in citrus fruit.
questions.explanation: Citrus fruits contain Vitamin C (also known as Ascorbic acid) is a large quantity. Due to the presence of Vitamin C and citric acid, citrus foods are sour in taste.

questions.question: In which of the following case, do you think the long term effects on your health are likely to be most unpleasant?
questions.explanation: Diseases are classified as acute or chronic, depending on their duration. Acute diseases such as common cold last only for a short duration. Chronic diseases last for a longer duration. Chronic diseases like jaundice cause long term effects on our health and harm the vital organs. Lice, acne, etc. do not have long term effects on our health but jaundice causes long term effects on our health.

questions.question: Which of the following is a chronic disease(s)?
questions.explanation: Both Asthma and Diabetes are chronic diseases as they last for a long duration and adversely affect the health of an individual. Diabetes is a group of metabolic disorders in which a person has high blood sugar levels over a prolonged period. Asthma is a common long-term inflammatory disease of the airways of the lungs. Acute diseases such as common cold last only for a short duration.

questions.question: Dietary requirement of protein increases:
questions.explanation: The dietary requirement of protein increases during lactation. Protein is called bodybuilding food. Protein is required in higher quantity during the lactation period after the parturition (the act of giving birth to young). Milk contains all the nutrients required for the growth of a baby.

questions.question: To take sufficient carbohydrate, one should take:
questions.explanation: Carbohydrate is present in rice and potato in large quantity. Carrots are rich in beta-carotene, which is converted into vitamin A in the liver. Groundnut contains a larger quantity of fat. It is the major oilseed of India.

questions.question: Find the correct pair of product and its ingredient:
questions.explanation: The correct pair is Whole grain bread – Roughage. Whole grain bread contains roughage. Roughage is another term for dietary fiber. Fiber comes from the parts of plant-based foods that the body cannot digest. Oat bran, oatmeal, whole-grain bread and whole-wheat pasta are good sources of fiber. Sugar provide energy, eggs provide proteins and butter provides insulation to the body.

questions.question: Which parameters to health are difficult to measure?

options.html: Both physical health and mental health

questions.explanation: Health is a state of being well enough to function well physically, mentally, and socially. Physical health can be measured easily. Mental health and social well-being are difficult to measure.

questions.question: To which of the following family, do folic acid and pentothenic acid belong?
questions.explanation: Folic acid (folate) is a type of vitamin B that's the key for cell growth, metabolism, and for pregnant women. Pantothenic acid, also called vitamin B5, is a water-soluble vitamin. Pantothenic acid is an essential nutrient. Animals require pantothenic acid to synthesize coenzyme-A (CoA), as well as to synthesize and metabolize proteins, carbohydrates, and fats. The anion is called pantothenate. Thus, folic acid and pantothenic acid belongs to the vitamin B complex group of vitamins.

questions.question: Mottling of teeth occurs due to presence of which element in drinking water?
questions.explanation:
Teeth’s mottling (also termed Dental fluorosis or mottled enamel) refers to a condition where the teeth get spotted as a result of taking water that has too much fluorine.
The presence of fluorine in drinking water due to contamination causes a mottling of teeth. It is an extremely common disorder, characterized by hypomineralization of the tooth enamel. Fluorine contamination is most common in hilly areas.

questions.question: Lipids in the diet:

options.html: Enable the absorption of some vitamins.

questions.explanation: Lipids are an important component of living cells. Together with carbohydrates and proteins, lipids are the main constituents of plant and animal cells. Lipids in the diet enable the absorption of some vitamins from the diet. They also provide energy in the absence of carbohydrates and proteins.

questions.question:
Which of the following is/are important for good health?

  1. Living in a clean space
  2. Living in a large and well-furnished house
  3. Social equality and harmony
  4. Balanced diet
questions.explanation:
A, C, and D are correct. Living in a large and well-furnished house is not important for individual health.
Health is a state of being well enough to function well physically, mentally. and socially. It depends on the surroundings or the environment. Living in a clean space under good economic conditions, and social equality and harmony are important for individual health. Good economic conditions are also needed so that an individual can work and earn, and arrange a balanced diet for nutrition. Mental and social well-being are difficult to measure.

questions.question:
Health is a state of:

  1. Physical well-being
  2. Social well-being
  3. Economic well-being
  4. Mental well-being
questions.explanation: A, B, and D are correct. Health is a state of being well enough to function well physically, mentally, and socially. Mental well-being and social well-being are difficult to measure.

  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Name the causal organism of kala-azar.
questions.explanation: Kala-azar or black fever or Dumdum fever is a dangerous disease caused by protozoa-Leishmania (genus of trypanosomes). Kala-azar is the most severe form of leishmaniasis. Plasmodium causes malaria.

questions.question: Which of the following is not the cause for the spread of infectious diseases?

options.html: By air directly or as droplets

questions.explanation: Infectious diseases spread from an infected person to a healthy person by air directly or as droplets, by intimate contact during sexual intercourse, and through vectors and carriers. Vaccination of diseases is not a cause for the spread of infectious diseases.

questions.question: Which of the following diseases is/are non-communicable?
questions.explanation:
The disease that cannot be transmitted from an infected individual to a healthy individual is called a non-communicable disease. Allergy is non-communicable.
Communicable diseases can be communicated i.e. they move from an infected person to someone else. Malaria, diarrhea, and tuberculosis (TB) are communicable diseases.

questions.question: A disease which begins abruptly and last only for a short duration is known as:
questions.explanation: Diseases are classified as acute or chronic, depending on their duration. The disease that begins abruptly and lasts only for a short duration is known as an acute disease. The common cold is the most common acute disease. Chronic and congenital diseases last for a long duration.

questions.question:
Match the following with the correct response:

(a) Hepatitis (i) Brain
(b) Japanese encephalitis (ii) Skin
(c) Dengue (iii) Platelets
(d) Fungal diseases (iv) Liver
questions.explanation:
Matched responses are as follows:

(a) Hepatitis (iv) Liver
(b) Japanese encephalitis (i) Brain
(c) Dengue (iii) Platelets
(d) Fungal diseases (ii) Skin
  • Hepatitis targets the liver.
  • Japanese encephalitis targets the brain.
  • Dengue leads to a drop in platelets.
  • Fungal diseases affect the skin.

questions.question:
Match the following with the correct response:

 (1) Acute disease  (A) Long lasting
 (2) Chronic disease  (B) Blocks biochemical pathways
 (3) Vaccine  (C) Suspension of dead or weak disease causing microbes
 (4) Antibiotics  (D) Short duration
questions.explanation:
Matched responses are as follows. Diseases are classified as acute or chronic, depending on their duration. Acute diseases such as common cold last only for a short duration. Chronic diseases last for a long duration. A vaccine is a biological preparation that provides active acquired immunity to a particular disease. A vaccine typically contains an agent that resembles a disease-causing microorganism and is often made from weakened or killed forms of the microbe. Antibiotics block the biochemical pathways that are used by microbes for the synthesis of substances/respiration, without affecting the biochemical pathways of human beings.

 (1) Acute disease  (D) Short duration
 (2) Chronic disease  (A) Long lasting
 (3) Vaccine  (C) Suspension of dead or weak disease causing microbes
 (4) Antibiotics  (B) Blocks biochemical pathways

questions.question: What is the full form of BCG?
questions.explanation: BCG stands for Bacillus Calmette Guerin. This vaccine prevents persons from tuberculosis (TB). The BCG vaccine was first used medically in 1921. It was developed in 1908 by Albert Calmette, a French physician and bacteriologist, and his assistant and later colleague, Camille Guérin, a veterinarian, who worked at the Institut Pasteur de Lille (Lille, France).

questions.question: Which of the following animals transmit rabies to human beings?
questions.explanation:
Rabies is mostly transmitted to human beings through the saliva of infected animals like rabid dogs and cats. Rabies is a viral disease caused by the rabies virus that causes inflammation of the brain in humans and other mammals. The virus enters the peripheral nervous system (PNS) and then travels along the nerves towards the central nervous system (CNS).
Infected bats, monkeys, raccoons, foxes, skunks, cattle, wolves, dogs, or cats provide the greatest risk to humans. Small rodents such as squirrels, hamsters, guinea pigs, gerbils, chipmunks, rats, and mice and lagomorphs like rabbits and hares are almost never found to be infected with rabies and are not known to transmit rabies to humans.

questions.question: Name the disease in which patient fears from water.
questions.explanation: The patient suffering from rabies in later stage develops a condition called hydrophobia in which patient fears from water. Hydrophobia (fear of water) is the historic name for rabies. It refers to a set of symptoms in the later stages of an infection in which the person has difficulty swallowing, shows panic when presented with liquids to drink, and cannot quench his or her thirst. Any mammal infected with the virus may demonstrate hydrophobia.

questions.question: Which organism causes Diarrhoea?
questions.explanation:
Diarrhoea is caused by bacterium salmonella along with Escherichia coli. Abnormally frequent discharge of semi-solid or fluid faeces occurs during Diarrhoea. 
Wuchereria bancrofti is a human parasitic roundworm that is the major cause of lymphatic filariasis (skin swelling).

questions.question: Which of the following disease is caused by bacteria?
questions.explanation: A number of diseases are caused by bacteria that include Diarrhoea, Typhoid, Tuberculosis etc. Typhoid is a bacterial infection caused by salmonellla typhi. A type of bacteria called mycobacterium tuberculosis causes Tuberculosis.

questions.question:
Find the correct pair:

  1. Typhoid – Salmonella typhii
  2. Tuberculosis – Plasmodium
  3. Malaria – Vibrio cholera
  4. Cholera – Mycobacterium tuberculosis
questions.explanation:
Typhoid is caused by bacterium Salmonella typhimurium. 
Tuberculosis is caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis.
Malaria is caused by plasmodium. 
Cholera is caused by Vibrio cholerae.

questions.question: Name the disease in which legs becomes paralysed.
questions.explanation: Poliomyelitis, often called polio or infantile paralysis, is an infectious disease caused by the polio virus, in which legs and other extremities become paralysed.

questions.question: Which one of the following is an incorrect sentence about Tuberculosis?

options.html: It is caused by Diplodocus tuberculosis.

questions.explanation: Tuberculosis (TB) is caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis. TB is a serious infection that affects the lungs and sometimes other parts of the body, such as the bones, joints, and kidneys. It can also cause meningitis. The bacteria release tuberculin toxin. The patient's sputum may contain blood.

questions.question: AIDS can be transmitted through:
questions.explanation: AIDS can be transmitted through sexual contact from one partner to another. It can also be transmitted through blood to blood contact with infected people or from an infected person to a healthy person through blood transfusion or from an infected mother to her baby during pregnancy or through breast-feeding. It is not transmitted through casual physical contact like handshake or hug.

This is Why Do We Fall Ill Test-03 for CBSE class 09 Science. Students are instructed o read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.

  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
